100.text: You’re not entirely sure that the sentient will participate in the duel until you make your first move, at which point they dodge just in time, using only the faintest of energy. They compliment your form before allowing you to attack again. This insightful-yet-infuriating process continues until they finally yield, offering you a special map as a prize. Gain item [b]Item 1263[/b]; the sentient highlights your current location (view page 20 in the Book of Vantages; you are in region K).

101.text: The beast is quite nimble for its size, but you overcome it. If you pay 1 boost, gain item [b]Item 1164[/b] from the carcass. If not, gain item [b]Item 1180[/b].

1014.action: [ec1c3b]FISH[-] for food (use only on a location with water)
1014.text: Roll 3 skill dice up to 3 times (always rolling all dice); roll 1 extra die if you pay a maximum of 1 boost from a food or flora. Based on number of [ec1c3b]overpower[-] in the final results, gain the following:

choices.outcome: You don’t catch anything other than a brief glimpse of fish. Gain 1 boost.
Catch a small but savory fish; gain 1 health.
Catch a medium-sized fish; gain 1 health and 1 boost.
Catch a large fish; gain item [b]Item 1226[/b].
Catch a monstrosity; gain item [b]Item 1232[/b].

1015.action: [ec1c3b]FISH[-] for food (use only on a location with water)
1015.text: Roll 3 skill dice up to 3 times (always rolling all dice); roll 1 extra die per boost you pay from this ship, plus 1 extra die if you pay a maximum of 1 boost from a food or flora. Based on number of [ec1c3b]overpower[-] in the final results, gain the following:

choices.outcome: You don’t catch anything other than a brief glimpse of fish. Gain 1 boost.
Catch a small but savory fish; gain 1 health.
Catch a medium-sized fish; gain 1 health and 1 boost.
Catch a large fish; gain item [b]Item 1226[/b].
Catch a monstrosity; gain item [b]Item 1232[/b].

1016.action: [ec1c3b]FISH[-] for food (use only on a location with water)
1016.text: Roll 3 skill dice up to 3 times (always rolling all dice); roll 1 extra die per boost you pay from this galleon, plus 1 extra die if you pay a maximum of 1 boost from a food or flora. Based on number of [ec1c3b]overpower[-] in the final results, gain the following:

Catch a medium-sized fish; gain 1 health and 1 boost.
Catch a large fish; gain item [b]Item 1226[/b].
Catch a monstrosity; gain item [b]Item 1232[/b].

102.text: After great resistance, the lock snaps open. Roll 5 skill dice; you may reroll each die once. If you have at least 3 of a kind, gain item [b]Item 1169[/b]. Instead, if you have at least one pair, gain item [b]Item 1207[/b]. Otherwise, the chest snaps shut on your finger; lose 1 health and gain 1 boost.

103.text: The animals scatter in fear as you rampage through their camp. Gain ability [b]Item 1467[/b] and go to interior [b]Location 549[/b].

104.text: As you reach for your weapon, the animal vanishes into thin air. You have a few seconds to study the details of your current location before there’s a flurry of movement in the grove, then the trap door slams shut. Go to [b]Location 511[/b] and continue.

105.text: The beast’s chirps turn to shrieks as it defends itself with massive talons, but you overcome it. If you pay 1 boost, gain item [b]Item 1160[/b] from the carcass. If not, gain item [b]Item 1186[/b].

106.text: You unearth the vegetation, shaking dirt from its roots. Gain flora [b]Item 1082[/b].

107.text: You begin to raze the device, and soon you find yourself in a scrap heap of metal and circuitry. The runecaster is now useless, but you’re impressed with how its creators took a naturally occurring phenomenon—runes— and produced a superior synthetic version. Gain 1 boost and your choice of a metal scrap (item [b]Item 1184[/b]) or a circuit board (item [b]Item 1190[/b]).

1070.action: [ec1c3b]PLUCK[-] a golden leaf to use as currency
1070.text: The metallic leaf shimmers in the sunlight. Gain $2 and continue.

108.text: Just in case the shield is booby trapped, you rampage through the grove, slashing at everything within reach. While you do, the remaining mushrooms target you with jets of lava. Roll 5 skill dice and gain all [2694d0]move[-] results. Lose 1 health unless you pay [2694d0]1 move[-]. Gain item [b]Item 939[/b].

109.text: Despite surprising the sentient, they reply as if you’re moving in slow motion, giving you combat tips as you parry. Eventually they collapse in faux defeat, complimenting your success while they falter. Gain flora [b]Item 1075[/b] and ability [b]Item 1474[/b].

110.text: The animals are horrified as you pluck a sacred flower from the ceremonial display. Gain flora [b]Item 1081[/b] and curse [b]Item 1388[/b].

111.text: The orb has little structural integrity after being broken, and you easily break off some useful pieces. Gain item [b]Item 1194[/b].

112.text: Caught out in the open, the beast is an easy target. If you pay 1 boost, gain item [b]Item 1162[/b] from the carcass. If not, gain item [b]Item 1181[/b].

1128.text: You create a small flame that briefly illuminates the void before burning out. Choose one: Refresh 1 challenge die from your grid, gain [a84fc0]1 look[-], or add 1 boost to an egg. Also, if you have a wood resource ([b]Item 1180[/b]), you may lose it to gain a bramble torch ([b]Item 1084[/b]).

113.action: [ec1c3b]PLUCK[-] the lotus, careful not to damage the roots or petals
113.text: You pluck the impressive flower. Gain flora [b]Item 1159[/b].

115.text: You knock the remaining shards to the floor and slip inside. Go to interior [b]Location 553[/b].

1156.action: [ec1c3b]FISH[-] for food (use only on a location with water)
1156.text: Roll 3 skill dice up to 3 times (always rolling all dice); roll 1 extra die if you pay a maximum of 1 boost from a food or flora. Based on number of [ec1c3b]overpower[-] in the final results, gain the following:

choices.outcome: You don’t catch anything other than a brief glimpse of fish. Gain 1 boost.
Catch a small but savory fish; gain 1 health.
Catch a medium-sized fish; gain 1 health and 1 boost.
Catch a large fish; gain item [b]Item 1226[/b].
Catch a monstrosity; gain item [b]Item 1232[/b].

116.text: You notice movement in the brush, so you set a basic trap and wait for it to spring. Set 1 challenge die to time, morale, or health to represent the trap (read options below). Then roll 3 other challenge dice; if any of them match the set die, gain the resulting benefit once (if none match, gain [ec1c3b]1 overpower[-] instead). These dice can come from the dice pool or the center; return them afterward.

choices.option: TIME (try to study an animal’s behavior)
choices.outcome: You observe the critter for a little while before setting it free. Gain 1 boost on your character and 1 boost on each leaf card.

choices.option: MORALE (try to gain an animal companion)
choices.outcome: The animal is scared but complacent. You will have to work to gain its trust. Gain animal [b]Item 845[/b].

choices.option: HEALTH (try to kill and eat an animal)
choices.outcome: The trap instantly kills the animal. Gain item [b]Item 1217[/b].

117.text: You crash into the crystal in an attempt to dislodge it, but it doesn’t budge. Gain 2 random skills; you may continue with the [fff349]take-[b]EXTRACT[/b][-] action.

118.text: You have only a few seconds to aim before the animal is gone. If you pay 1 boost, gain item [b]Item 1161[/b] from the carcass. If not, gain item [b]Item 1212[/b].

119.text: You unearth the vegetation, shaking dirt from the roots. The nearby sentient sees what you’ve done and smites you. Gain flora [b]Item 1080[/b] and curse [b]Item 1388[/b].

012.action: [ec1c3b]RAID[-] the shipwreck of the deadliest item within
012.text: Dislodge the sniper rifle (gain item [b]Item 1153[/b]), then exit to [b]Location 649[/b].

120.text: Roll 3 skill dice up to 3 times (always rolling all dice); roll 1 extra die if you pay a maximum of 1 boost from a food or flora. Based on number of [ec1c3b]overpower[-] in the final results, gain the following:

choices.outcome: You don’t catch anything other than a brief glimpse of fish. Gain 1 boost.
Catch a small but savory fish; gain 1 health.
Catch a medium-sized fish; gain 1 health and 1 boost.
Catch a large fish; gain item [b]Item 1226[/b].
Catch a monstrosity; gain item [b]Item 1232[/b].

121.text: Roll 3 skill dice up to 3 times (always rolling all dice); roll 1 extra die if you pay a maximum of 1 boost from a food or flora. Based on number of [ec1c3b]overpower[-] in the final results, gain the following:

choices.outcome: You don’t catch anything other than a brief glimpse of fish. Gain 1 boost.
Catch a small but savory fish; gain 1 health.
Catch a medium-sized fish; gain 1 health and 1 boost.
Catch a large fish; gain item [b]Item 1226[/b].
Catch a monstrosity; gain item [b]Item 1232[/b].

122.text: The Traveler actually seems relieved that you’re not using the station for its intended purpose, though they’re also a bit concerned that you’ve created a tool for destruction. Gain item [b]Item 1038[/b].

123.text: Despite the water, you manage to cause the lorestone to fall onto its side. Embedded into the bottom half is a purple crystal. Gain item [b]Item 1161[/b].

124.text: Lose 1 health and go to interior [b]Location 554[/b].

125.text: As you suspected, even with the first tunnel cut off as an option, the artifact is still accessible via one of the other two tunnels. If you pay 3 boost, you discover a path to the artifact (gain item [b]Item 998[/b] and perform no other location actions here). If not, the mystery of the artifact remains unsolved, and the labyrinth vanishes; gain 1 boost.

1253.text: Choose one: Refresh 1 challenge die from your grid, gain [a84fc0]1 look[-], or add 1 boost to an egg. Also, if you have a wood resource ([b]Item 1180[/b]), you may lose it to gain a bramble torch ([b]Item 1084[/b]).

126.text: After great resistance, the lock snaps open. Roll 5 skill dice; you may reroll each die once. If you have at least 3 of a kind, gain item [b]Item 1164[/b]. Instead, if you have at least one pair, gain item [b]Item 895[/b]. Otherwise, gain $4.

1265.action: [ec1c3b]DUEL[-] one of your sentients in a card and dice game (if you don't have a sentient, select another action)
1265.text: The sentient welcomes the challenge and awes you with their shuffling skills. Place card [b]Location 798[/b] on the table (it’s a large card) and follow its instructions.Variant: Quick Skirm (ignore card 798).

1274.text: A water sentient slumps against the door of the embassy, exhausted from their journey. They are wearing a device that allows them to breathe on land. Soon afterward, two massive water soldiers appear outside the embassy, demanding that you release the refugee. In the fight that ensues, you leave no doubt about your commitment to safe harbor. Gain sentient [b]Item 1356[/b] and 1 morale.

1275.action: [ec1c3b]WISH[-] for power (pay 1 boost from this structure; perform this action at most once per game)

1276.action: [ec1c3b]DUEL[-] a sentient from the crowd in front of a live audience
1276.text: A sentient welcomes the challenge and joins you on stage. Place card [b]Location 798[/b] on the table (it’s a large card) and follow its instructions.Variant: Quick Skirm (ignore card 798).

1277.action: [ec1c3b]DUEL[-] one of your sentients in a card and dice game (if you don't have a sentient, select another action)

1279.action: [ec1c3b]DEFEND[-] your stable from predators and poachers
1279.text: Nothing makes you madder than something or someone who would harm the animals you love. Gain 1 morale and 1 boost on your character.

128.text: The bulky sentient nods in appreciation of your courage, but they easily block your best charge. They declare themselves the keeper of the blade and invite you to solve a combat challenge to see if you’re worthy. Gain [a84fc0]1 look[-] and [ec1c3b]1 overpower[-].

1280.action: [ec1c3b]DUEL[-] one of your students in a card and dice game (if you don't have a sentient, select another action)
1280.text: The student welcomes the distraction from class. Place card [b]Location 798[/b] on the table (it’s a large card) and follow its instructions.Variant: Quick Skirm (ignore card 798).

1285.text: Either gain 1 boost on this structure or gain 2 boost on an egg.

1286.action: [ec1c3b]TARGET[-] a tasty-looking critter within range of the hunting tower
1286.text: If there is 1 boost on this structure, roll a challenge die and gain the result (if any; the die remains in the supply). If 2 boost, gain 1 health. If 3 boost, gain item [b]Item 1217[/b]. All boost remain on this structure.

1287.text: Pair your current region (see [a84fc0]look[-] storybook) with its underground location (page 47 in the Book of Vantages), then go there.

1288.action: [ec1c3b]PLUCK[-] a flora from the garden (if there are no flora cards tucked under this structure, choose a different action)
1288.text: Gain a flora card tucked under this structure and place it in your grid.

1289.action: [ec1c3b]DUEL[-] one of your sentients in a card and dice game (if you don’t have a sentient, select another action)

129.text: You strike deep into the base of the spire, and your blow reveals that the stone is hollow. Go to interior [b]Location 555[/b].

1290.action: [ec1c3b]DUEL[-] one of your sentients in a card and dice game (if you don’t have a sentient, select another action)

1291.action: [ec1c3b]PLUCK[-] a flora from the garden (if there are no flora cards tucked under this structure, choose a different action)
1291.text: Gain a flora card tucked under this structure and place it in your grid.

1292.text: The outpost is a base for conquering this world, and you use it to prepare for war. If there is 1 boost on this structure, gain item [b]Item 1416[/b]. If 2 boost, gain item [b]Item 1151[/b]. If 3 boost, gain item [b]Item 1024[/b]. All boost remain on this structure.

1293.text: Gain an experience card ([2694d0]captain[-]: [b]Item 1454[/b]; [a84fc0]navigator[-]: [b]Item 1455[/b]; [fd8c46]scholar[-]: [b]Item 1456[/b]; [0b955e]medic[-]: [b]Item 1457[/b]; [fff349]engineer[-]: [b]Item 1458[/b]; [ec1c3b]marine[-]: [b]Item 1459[/b]). If you already have your experience card, instead gain 1 boost on your character.

013.action: [ec1c3b]RAID[-] the cottage of its largest item
013.text: You muster the strength to carry the large bedroll. Gain item [b]Item 1040[/b] and slip outside (exit to [b]Location 719[/b]).

130.text: The sentient on the right is relieved to take a break after losing several times in a row. Place card [b]Location 798[/b] on the table (it’s a large card) and follow its instructions, using card [b]Item 1520[/b] as the opponent’s card.Variant: Quick Skirm (ignore card 798).

You can duel the sentient again in the future only if you win.Variant: Quick Skirm (ignore card 798).

137.text: Several meters underground, you feel a sense of satisfaction and strength. Gain 1 health and 1 boost. You may either stop or continue with an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆2-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result only):

choices.outcome: A solid material halts your progress, and you excavate a large crystal (item [b]Item 1161[/b]). You may either stop or continue with an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆3-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result):
The earth beneath your feet gives way, sending you tumbling into the depths. Go to cave [b]Location 086[/b].

138.action: [ec1c3b]PLUCK[-] the lotus, careful not to damage the roots or petals
138.text: You pluck the graceful flower. Gain flora [b]Item 1159[/b].

139.text: You manage to cause the lorestone to fall onto its side. Embedded into the bottom half is an orange crystal. Gain item [b]Item 1162[/b].

140.text: You notice movement in the long grass, so you set a basic trap and wait for it to spring. Set 1 challenge die to time, morale, or health to represent the trap (read options below). Then roll 3 other challenge dice; if any of them match the set die, gain the resulting benefit once (if none match, gain [ec1c3b]1 overpower[-] instead). These dice can come from the dice pool or the center; return them afterward.

choices.option: TIME (try to study an animal’s behavior)
choices.outcome: You observe the critter for a little while before setting it free. Gain 1 boost on your character and 1 boost on each energy card.

choices.option: MORALE (try to gain an animal companion)
choices.outcome: The animal is scared but complacent. You will have to work to gain its trust. Gain animal [b]Item 823[/b].

choices.option: HEALTH (try to kill and eat an animal)
choices.outcome: The trap instantly kills the animal. Gain item [b]Item 1211[/b].

1409.text: Choose one: Refresh 1 challenge die from your grid, gain [a84fc0]1 look[-], or add 1 boost to an egg.

141.text: Several meters underground, you feel a sense of satisfaction and strength. Gain 1 health and 1 boost. You may either stop or continue with an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆2-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result):

choices.outcome: A solid material halts your progress, and you excavate a large crystal. Gain item [b]Item 1163[/b].

142.text: The wreckage is an abundant source of scrap metal, and you twist it into something useful for catching little critters. Gain item [b]Item 923[/b].

143.text: Sparks fly as you carve a large gash in the ship, revealing precious cargo—your power armor. You may replace your character with the corresponding card ([2694d0]captain[-]: [b]Item 1447[/b]; [a84fc0]navigator[-]: [b]Item 1448[/b]; [fd8c46]scholar[-]: [b]Item 1449[/b]; [0b955e]medic[-]: [b]Item 1450[/b]; [fff349]engineer[-]: [b]Item 1451[/b]; [ec1c3b]marine[-]: [b]Item 1452[/b]); transfer all boost from your original character onto the new card. If you already have your power armor, instead gain 3 boost.

144.text: The sentient nods appreciatively of your honorable approach. They maintain an erect posture throughout the duel, darting forward to prod but not hurt. Eventually they concede, deeming you worthy enough to accompany them to the northeast to wrest control of the elemental there. Gain sentient [b]Item 1352[/b].

145.text: The sentient crashes to the ground, flowers springing to life everywhere they touch. “Why would you do this?” they gasp. “You could have helped your friends.” You shrug, grab a flower, and walk away. Gain flora [b]Item 1080[/b] and curse [b]Item 1395[/b].

146.action: [ec1c3b]DUEL[-] the sentient in a card and dice game
146.text: The energy sentient eagerly reveals a vast collection of cards. Place card [b]Location 798[/b] on the table (it’s a large card) and follow its instructions, using card [b]Item 1519[/b] as the opponent’s card.Variant: Quick Skirm (ignore card 798).

1461.text: Roll 4 skill dice up to 4 times (always rolling all dice); roll 1 extra die if you pay a maximum of 1 boost from a food or flora. Based on number of [ec1c3b]overpower[-] in the final results, gain the following:

choices.outcome: You don’t catch anything other than a brief glimpse of fish. Gain 1 boost.
Catch a small but savory fish; gain 1 health.
Catch a medium-sized fish; gain 1 health and 1 boost.
Catch a large fish; gain item [b]Item 1226[/b].
Catch a monstrosity; gain item [b]Item 1232[/b].

147.action: [ec1c3b]TARGET[-] a low-flying bird-like creature as it glides nearby
147.text: One of the creatures sadly falls lifeless along the shore. Gain item [b]Item 1186[/b] and continue.

1473.text: You pay attention to signs in nature to identify an animal, conceal your presence, and prepare to strike. The animal goes down in a heap, a soulstone glowing within the carcass. Roll 1 skill die and gain the matching crystal ([2694d0]blue[-]: [b]Item 1160[/b]; [a84fc0]purple[-]: [b]Item 1161[/b]; [fd8c46]orange[-]: [b]Item 1162[/b]; [0b955e]green[-]: [b]Item 1163[/b]; [fff349]yellow[-]: [b]Item 1164[/b]; [ec1c3b]red[-]: [b]Item 1165[/b]); if you already have a crystal in that color, gain a curse instead (curse [b]Item 1392[/b] if land; curse [b]Item 1385[/b] if underwater).

148.text: Roll 3 skill dice up to 3 times (always rolling all dice); roll 1 extra die if you pay a maximum of 1 boost from a food or flora. Based on number of [ec1c3b]overpower[-] in the final results, gain the following:

158.text: The sentient offers no resistance, acting as if they deserve the pain. You can’t bear to continue, so you ease them to the ground. Gain 1 boost and continue.

159.text: The odds are stacked against you, and even without weapons the sentients fight with reckless abandon. Finally you pin them in the shallow water and they surrender. Gain item [b]Item 882[/b] and 1 morale.

1592.action: [ec1c3b]CONQUER[-] part of a city (on a city location)
1592.text: It requires great strategic planning, a large show of force, and pinpoint execution to conquer a city. Place 1 token on this mission. If there are 3 tokens here, the mission is complete (read [b]Location 1592[/b] in the Book of Secrets.). This action may be used on different cities, with the mission completed when the [ec1c3b]CONQUER[-] action is performed 3 times on the same city.

016.text: As you approach the vegetation, it releases a great burst of scalding liquid as a defense mechanism. Roll 2 skill dice and gain all [2694d0]move[-] results. Suffer 1 heat damage unless you pay [2694d0]1 move[-]. Gain flora [b]Item 1077[/b] and continue.

160.text: The guards seem excited to have a challenger, but you (barely) manage to overwhelm them. Go to interior [b]Location 562[/b].

161.text: The sentient grunts their appreciation. After a few minutes, they gesture for you to stand back as scalding lava bursts through the crust. They guide you down to the water and ask if you’d like some company on your journey. Gain sentient [b]Item 1315[/b].

162.text: Intimidating guards try to stop you at the main entrance, but you push them aside. Go to city [b]Location 525[/b].

163.text: Their shell not only protects them, but it also bursts into flame whenever you get too close. You wait for the right moment and find its weak spot. If you pay 1 boost, gain item [b]Item 1163[/b] from the carcass. If not, gain item [b]Item 1182[/b].

164.text: You destroy the eye, and an amulet falls out of it. A furious sentient crashes into you, defending their mother. Roll 3 skill dice and gain all [ec1c3b]overpower[-] results. Lose 1 health unless you pay [ec1c3b]1 overpower[-]. Gain item [b]Item 1177[/b].

1642.text: You help the water sentients conquer the location, learning some combat techniques from them in the process. Place 1 token on quest [b]Item 1642[/b] to track your progress, then gain [ec1c3b]1 overpower[-] per token on the quest. You may not perform any actions on this location other than moving away. If there are now 3 tokens on this quest, remove them and read [b]Location 1642[/b] in the Book of Secrets..

165.text: Sparks fly as you carve a large gash in the ship, revealing precious cargo—your power armor. You may replace your character with the corresponding card ([2694d0]captain[-]: [b]Item 1447[/b]; [a84fc0]navigator[-]: [b]Item 1448[/b]; [fd8c46]scholar[-]: [b]Item 1449[/b]; [0b955e]medic[-]: [b]Item 1450[/b]; [fff349]engineer[-]: [b]Item 1451[/b]; [ec1c3b]marine[-]: [b]Item 1452[/b]); transfer all boost from your original character onto the new card. If you already have your power armor, instead gain 3 boost.

1659.text: On quest [b]Item 1659[/b], add X tokens, then remove tokens equal to the number of dice you didn’t place (including blanks). At the end of this action, if there are at least 9 tokens on the quest, read [b]Location 1659[/b] in the Book of Secrets..

166.text: You cautiously cut down the tree, avoiding the large pockets of lava under its thin bark. As you do, the occupant of the house runs outside, shouting at you. Apparently they were using the tree to guard against trespassers. Gain flora [b]Item 1077[/b] and curse [b]Item 1394[/b].

1660.text: Roll 3 skill dice up to 3 times (always rolling all dice); roll 1 extra die if you pay a maximum of 1 boost from a food or flora. Based on number of [ec1c3b]overpower[-] in the final results, gain the following:

choices.outcome: You don’t catch anything other than a brief glimpse of fish. Gain 1 boost to your character; you may perform this [ec1c3b]overpower-[b]FISH[/b][-] action again on a future turn.
Catch a small but savory fish; gain 1 health and you may read [b]Location 1660[/b] in the Book of Secrets..
Catch a medium-sized fish; gain 2 health and you may read [b]Location 1660[/b] in the Book of Secrets..
Catch a large fish; gain item [b]Item 1226[/b] and you may read [b]Location 1660[/b] in the Book of Secrets..

167.text: The sentient accepts your challenge even though it seems like all they actually want to do is lie down. They wield an impressive flamethrower, forcing you to choose carefully your moments to strike. Roll 3 skill dice and gain all [2694d0]move[-] results. Suffer 1 heat damage unless you pay [2694d0]1 move[-]. Gain item [b]Item 962[/b].

1674.text: The knight has the upper hand throughout the tussle, but you finally get them to submit after cornering them against the rock wall. Choose one:

choices.option: Let the knight plead their case
choices.outcome: The knight explains that despite living a life of violence, they are trying to reform the ways of their warmongering brethren. That’s why they left the deep sea with the Blue Blade. If you agree not to collect the bounty, they pledge their loyalty to you (lose quest [b]Item 1674[/b], gain sentient [b]Item 1357[/b], and go north to [b]Location 133[/b]). If you disagree, read the next option instead.

choices.option: Turn in the knight to collect the bounty
choices.outcome: The knight shouts that you’re making a huge mistake as they’re taken away by water soldiers. Gain $15 and go either east to [b]Location 129[/b] or west to [b]Location 127[/b]. Then rotate quest [b]Item 1674[/b] and add it to your grid.

168.text: The fire elemental paces before you with mild curiosity. First, gain curse [b]Item 1394[/b]. Then find elemental combat cards [b]Item 1541[/b]–[b]Item 1544[/b] and compile them into a cohesive image. Choose one of those cards and continue to perform an action on it.

1680.text: Three sentients charge across the bridge, brandishing weapons and clever wordplay. You throw one into the muddy water, another you knock out cold, and you lock eyes with the last and don’t blink until they decide you aren’t worth the trouble. Add 1 token to quest [b]Item 1680[/b].

1687.action: [ec1c3b]DUEL[-] one of your sentients in a card and dice game (if you don't have a sentient, select another action)
1687.text: The sentient welcomes the challenge and awes you with their dice-rolling dexterity. Place card [b]Location 798[/b] on the table (it’s a large card) and follow its instructions.Variant: Quick Skirm (ignore card 798).

1688.action: [ec1c3b]DUEL[-] one of your sentients in a card and dice game (if you don't have a sentient, select another action)
1688.text: The sentient welcomes the challenge and awes you with their dice-rolling dexterity. Place card [b]Location 798[/b] on the table (it’s a large card) and follow its instructions.Variant: Quick Skirm (ignore card 798).

1689.action: [ec1c3b]DUEL[-] one of your sentients in a card and dice game (if you don't have a sentient, select another action)
1689.text: The sentient welcomes the challenge and awes you with their dice-rolling dexterity. Place card [b]Location 798[/b] on the table (it’s a large card) and follow its instructions.Variant: Quick Skirm (ignore card 798).

169.action: [ec1c3b]DESTROY[-] a weapon or a coin in the sacred lava
169.text: As onlookers and the Traveler watch, choose an object (a coin or item) to lose in the lava. Choose one:

choices.outcome: Sentients look a bit confused, but they appreciate the gesture. Gain 1 morale and [2694d0]1 move[-].
Sentients applaud your participation in their tradition; gain spell [b]Item 1367[/b] and [2694d0]1 move[-].
Gain [2694d0]1 move[-].

1690.action: [ec1c3b]DUEL[-] one of your sentients in a card and dice game (if you don't have a sentient, select another action)
1690.text: The sentient welcomes the challenge and awes you with their dice-rolling dexterity. Place card [b]Location 798[/b] on the table (it’s a large card) and follow its instructions.Variant: Quick Skirm (ignore card 798).

1691.action: [ec1c3b]DUEL[-] one of your sentients in a card and dice game (if you don't have a sentient, select another action)
1691.text: The sentient welcomes the challenge and awes you with their dice-rolling dexterity. Place card [b]Location 798[/b] on the table (it’s a large card) and follow its instructions.Variant: Quick Skirm (ignore card 798).

1692.action: [ec1c3b]DUEL[-] one of your sentients in a card and dice game (if you don't have a sentient, select another action)
1692.text: The sentient welcomes the challenge and awes you with their dice-rolling dexterity. Place card [b]Location 798[/b] on the table (it’s a large card) and follow its instructions.Variant: Quick Skirm (ignore card 798).

1694.text: Roll X skill dice. You are trying to have more invaders ([ec1c3b]overpower[-] results) than defenders at each castle entrance ([2694d0]move[-] results). For each [a84fc0]look[-] result, you may reroll 1 result not mentioned in this description. At the end of this action, if there are more invaders than defenders, read [b]Location 1694[/b] in the Book of Secrets..

1696.action: [ec1c3b]PLUCK[-] a crystal from the tree (remove 1 boost from this card)
1696.text: Roll a skill die. Based on the result, gain a crystal ([2694d0]blue[-]: [b]Item 1160[/b]; [a84fc0]purple[-]: [b]Item 1161[/b]; [fd8c46]orange[-]: [b]Item 1162[/b]; [0b955e]green[-]: [b]Item 1163[/b]; [fff349]yellow[-]: [b]Item 1164[/b]; [ec1c3b]red[-]: [b]Item 1165[/b]).

1698.text: Gain item [b]Item 1224[/b] with only 1 boost on it.

017.text: The monster puts up a valiant struggle, but you overcome them. You pocket the elixir you find in their claw (gain item [b]Item 864[/b]). Go deeper into the planet to cave [b]Location 044[/b].

170.text: The rock is incredibly strong for how light it is. You stick a metal rod in it and give it a worthy name. Gain item [b]Item 950[/b].

1701.action: [ec1c3b]DUEL[-] one of your sentients in a card and dice game (if you don't have a sentient, select another action)
1701.text: The sentient welcomes the challenge and awes you with their dice-rolling dexterity. Place card [b]Location 798[/b] on the table (it’s a large card) and follow its instructions.Variant: Quick Skirm (ignore card 798).

1705.action: [ec1c3b]TARGET[-] the rescue ship as it attempts to land so you can achieve victory on your own terms
1705.text: You attack the ship as soon as it’s within range, sending it crashing into the planet. The wreckage reveals precious cargo—your power armor. You may replace your character with the corresponding card ([2694d0]captain[-]: [b]Item 1447[/b]; [a84fc0]navigator[-]: [b]Item 1448[/b]; [fd8c46]scholar[-]: [b]Item 1449[/b]; [0b955e]medic[-]: [b]Item 1450[/b]; [fff349]engineer[-]: [b]Item 1451[/b]; [ec1c3b]marine[-]: [b]Item 1452[/b]); transfer all boost from your original character onto the new card. If you already have your power armor, instead gain 3 boost. Lose destiny [b]Item 1705[/b], gain destiny [b]Item 1600[/b] (place it in the center), and proceed with the next player’s turn.

171.text: The orb is weakened by exposure to the lava and shatters across the ground, much to the chagrin of the stone sentient who was inside. Gain item [b]Item 1194[/b] and curse [b]Item 1389[/b].

172.text: You pull yourself onto the deck, expecting to meet at least a little resistance for your piracy. However, the leaf sentients aboard seem excited by the opportunity to empty their pockets, each offering more than the next before they drop you off on the far shore. Gain $9; you may pay [2694d0]1 move[-] or [a84fc0]1 look[-] to go northwest to [b]Location 251[/b].

173.text: You begin to raze the device, and soon you find yourself in a scrap heap of metal and circuitry. The runecaster is now useless, but you’re impressed with how its creators took a naturally occurring phenomenon—runes— and produced a superior synthetic version. Gain 1 boost and your choice of a metal scrap (item [b]Item 1184[/b]) or a circuit board (item [b]Item 1190[/b]).

174.text: The judges gasp and welcome you to participate in the trial. Continue with the [fff349]take-[b]ENDURE[/b][-] action.

175.text: Luckily, the sentient is all style and no substance, and you have no problem halting their attack. They weakly offer you $5 if you agree to go away (go to an adjacent location; north is [b]Location 180[/b]). If you reject their offer, gain item [b]Item 867[/b] and continue.

176.action: [ec1c3b]FORCE[-] a sentient out of the twister
176.text: The sentient crashes to the ground, their wing pinned beneath your knee. “You caught me!” they exclaim. “We shouldn’t have dined and dashed, but wind people like us use the barter system. Here, I’ve transferred credits to the tavern to cover the party. That’s why you’re here, right?” Gain 2 boost.

178.action: [ec1c3b]PLUCK[-] a flower in defiance of the sentient
178.text: You unearth the towering vegetation, shaking dirt from its roots. The sentient is furious. Gain flora [b]Item 1076[/b] and curse [b]Item 1387[/b].

179.text: You unearth the vegetation, shaking sand from the roots. A nearby sentient sees what you’ve done and smites you. Gain flora [b]Item 1076[/b] and curse [b]Item 1387[/b].

018.text: You unearth the vegetation, shaking dirt from its roots. Gain flora [b]Item 1060[/b].

180.text: You quickly realize that for this elder, dueling is more of a dance battle than a physical altercation. They take joy in your awkwardness but commend you on being a good sport. Gain sentient [b]Item 1308[/b].

181.text: In a swift, well-practiced motion, the sentients adopt a defensive stance between you and the treasure chest. They’re not strong, but they’re incredibly fast. Gain item [b]Item 1021[/b].

182.text: For all their posturing, the sentient is an inexplicably weak fighter. They’re fast, though, and it takes a few minutes to strike the winning blow. When you do, you empty their pockets for a reward. Gain item [b]Item 867[/b] and $2.

183.action: [ec1c3b]FIGHT[-] the sentient in a dance-off (you may not use weapons)
183.text: Weapons have no place on the dance floor. Even though the sentient is a far better dancer than you, they appreciate the effort and give you some pointers. Gain 1 morale and 1 boost; continue.

184.text: The queen gasps and welcomes you to participate in the trial. Continue with the [0b955e]help-[b]ENLIVEN[/b][-] action.

185.text: The stone crashes to the ground, much to the dismay of sentients in the area. Despite the disrespectful act, you’re happy to see that a flute detached from the column. Gain item [b]Item 949[/b] and curse [b]Item 1387[/b].

186.text: No one tries to stop you—the town is open to outsiders. Calm down and go to city [b]Location 518[/b].

187.text: The locals don’t back down from the incursion, and you must fight your way through them. Roll 2 skill dice and gain all [2694d0]move[-] results. Lose 1 health unless you pay [2694d0]1 move[-]. When the raid is over, if you pay 2 boost, you discover a way to activate the artifact (gain item [b]Item 1003[/b] and perform no other location actions here). If not, the mystery of the artifact remains unsolved, and it vanishes from your grasp; gain 1 boost.

188.action: [ec1c3b]RELIEVE[-] the turbine’s owner of their misery and scrap the turbine
188.text: There’s a sense of relief amidst the sentient’s tears. They get a fresh start, and you get some scrap metal. Gain item [b]Item 1184[/b] and 1 morale.

189.text: Roll 3 skill dice up to 3 times (always rolling all dice); roll 1 extra die if you pay a maximum of 1 boost from a food or flora. Based on number of [ec1c3b]overpower[-] in the final results, gain the following:

choices.outcome: You don’t catch anything other than a brief glimpse of fish. Gain 1 boost.
Catch a small but savory fish; gain 1 health.
Catch a medium-sized fish; gain 1 health and 1 boost.
Catch a large fish; gain item [b]Item 1226[/b].
Catch a monstrosity; gain item [b]Item 1232[/b].

190.text: Stumbling and scraping against the rough tunnel walls in the pitch-black tunnel, you smash your shins into a sharp rock. You can instantly feel a bulbous bruise blossom. Lose 1 health and gain [a84fc0]1 look[-]; go to cave [b]Location 036[/b].

191.text: You begin to raze the device, and soon you find yourself in a scrap heap of metal and circuitry. The runecaster is now useless, but you’re impressed with how its creators took a naturally occurring phenomenon—runes— and produced a superior synthetic version. Gain 1 boost and your choice of a metal scrap (item [b]Item 1184[/b]) or a circuit board (item [b]Item 1190[/b]).

192.action: [ec1c3b]CLEAVE[-] the closest flag in half while making aggressive eye contact with the sentient
192.text: The sentient shakes their fist in anger but doesn’t confront you. Gain curse [b]Item 1387[/b] and continue.

193.action: [ec1c3b]DIG[-] into the grassy patch at your feet
193.text: Several meters underground, you feel a sense of satisfaction and strength. Gain 1 health and 1 boost. You may either stop or continue with an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆2-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result only):

choices.outcome: A solid material halts your progress, and you excavate a huppeldepup. Gain item [b]Item 1102[/b]. You may either stop or continue with an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆3-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result only):

A rich, dark layer of sediment is prime for deep fungus. Gain item [b]Item 1136[/b]. You may either stop or continue with an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆4-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result):
The earth beneath your feet gives way, sending you tumbling into the depths. Go to cave [b]Location 090[/b].

194.text: You’re out of your element in this windy, magical duel, but the opposing sentient didn’t sign up for a 2-on-1 battle, and they soon fly away. The other sentient, tired from the clash, thanks you with a gift. Gain item [b]Item 981[/b].

195.action: [ec1c3b]DIG[-] into the grassy patch at your feet
195.text: Several meters underground, you feel a sense of satisfaction and strength. Gain 1 health and 1 boost. You may either stop or continue with an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆2-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result):

choices.outcome: The earth beneath your feet gives way, sending you tumbling into the depths. Go to cave [b]Location 036[/b].

196.text: Sparks fly as you carve a large gash in the ship, revealing precious cargo—your power armor. You may replace your character with the corresponding card ([2694d0]captain[-]: [b]Item 1447[/b]; [a84fc0]navigator[-]: [b]Item 1448[/b]; [fd8c46]scholar[-]: [b]Item 1449[/b]; [0b955e]medic[-]: [b]Item 1450[/b]; [fff349]engineer[-]: [b]Item 1451[/b]; [ec1c3b]marine[-]: [b]Item 1452[/b]); transfer all boost from your original character onto the new card. If you already have your power armor, instead gain 3 boost.

197.text: The sentient begs for mercy as you pin them to the ground. All they want is to finish their cartography project. You let them up, and they place the flag and fly away. Gain 1 morale.

198.text: The orb is surprisingly durable, but you manage to crack and break it. A pink sentient darts away, angry at the intrusion. Gain item [b]Item 1194[/b].

199.text: The sentient is enraged. “You imbecile! This isn’t how you treat a lock—you must delicately pick it open. You’ve ruined everything.” They bump into you as they move away, and your purse feels a little lighter afterward. Lose $2, then roll 5 skill dice to reveal the contents of the chest; you may reroll any/all dice up to 2 times. If you have at least 3 of a kind, gain item [b]Item 1036[/b]. Instead, if you have at least one pair, gain item [b]Item 1161[/b]. Otherwise, the chest attempts to eat you; lose 1 health.

020.text: You ignore the puzzle trap and pummel the barrier, but it re-forms as soon as you shatter it. Refresh up to 2 challenge dice from your grid and continue.

200.text: With a solid kick, a large chunk tumbles down the cliff. It clears a partial path through the valley below that requires slightly less navigation. Gain 1 boost and [a84fc0]1 look[-]; continue.

201.text: Your unexpected attack knocks the sentient off balance, and they land hard on the turf. Their dignity is more bruised than anything else. You blame it on the wind and help them up, and they give you a few coins: Gain $3.

202.text: The sentient grins playfully—they’re an engineer, so they don’t often have opportunities for a good tussle. Afterwards, they ask if you’d like company in your journey. If you say yes, gain sentient [b]Item 1337[/b]. Otherwise, gain [ec1c3b]1 overpower[-].

203.text: The wind adds to the difficulty of the fight, but the sentient is all bark and no bite. As they tumble to the depths below, you hear a cheer from the inside of the building, and an exuberant sentient emerges. They embrace you and thank you for freeing them. Gain sentient [b]Item 1349[/b].

204.text: The result of you entering the building is based on the current occupancy status. Proceed if you already know this status; if not, roll a challenge die to randomly determine the status:

choices.option: No one is home (blank challenge die)
choices.outcome: Go to interior [b]Location 556[/b]; on your next turn, you may not choose the [fd8c46]engage-[b]TRADE[/b][-] action there.

The house is occupied. Your surprise entrance sends the family running (gain curse [b]Item 1391[/b] and go to interior [b]Location 556[/b]; on your next turn, you may not choose the [fd8c46]engage-[b]TRADE[/b][-] action there).

205.action: [ec1c3b]PLUCK[-] the lotus, careful not to damage the roots or petals
205.text: You pluck the impressively adaptable flower. Gain flora [b]Item 1159[/b].

206.text: The guards try to stop you, but you push them aside. Go to city [b]Location 522[/b].

207.text: The king gasps and welcomes you to participate in the trial. Continue with the [fff349]take-[b]BYPASS[/b][-] action.

208.text: The orb is surprisingly durable, but you manage to crack and break it. Gain item [b]Item 1194[/b].

209.text: You tell yourself that you’re hunting the animal, but it is just as much hunting you. You barely overcome it. If you pay 1 boost, gain item [b]Item 1165[/b] from the carcass. If not, gain item [b]Item 1184[/b].

021.action: [ec1c3b]SMASH[-] a stalactite to try to form a crude bridge across the gap
021.text: Your attempts at brute force are fruitless. Refresh up to 2 challenge dice from your grid and continue.

210.text: As you get close to the object, you can feel all of your metal objects pulling you toward it. You quickly slice through it, and as it breaks clean from the base, you can feel the magnetic force instantly dissipate. Gain 2 morale; from now on the cost to move north from here is 1.

211.action: [ec1c3b]DUEL[-] the sentient in a card and dice game
211.text: The metal sentient eagerly reveals a vast collection of cards. Place card [b]Location 798[/b] on the table (it’s a large card) and follow its instructions, using card [b]Item 1523[/b] as the opponent’s card. If you win, the sentient enjoys playing with you so much that they offer to join you. Gain sentient [b]Item 1336[/b].Variant: Quick Skirm (ignore card 798).

212.text: Your ignorance of this species leaves you at a distinct disadvantage against the beast, and you are fortunate to overcome it. If you pay 1 boost, gain item [b]Item 1162[/b] from the carcass. If not, gain item [b]Item 1184[/b].

213.text: The solid, cold ground makes digging quite a challenge, but finally you prevail. Go to interior [b]Location 513[/b] and gain 1 health.

214.text: After almost slipping off the narrow ledge, you crash into an ironclad sentinel. Lose 1 health and gain [fd8c46]1 engage[-]; go to cave [b]Location 034[/b].

215.text: You knock the remaining shards from the pane and slip inside the tavern. Go to interior [b]Location 557[/b].

216.text: With the high-security building unyielding to your might, you instead turn your attention to the pretty flowers. You unearth the vegetation, shaking dirt from its roots. A nearby sentient sees what you’ve done and smites you. Gain flora [b]Item 1055[/b] and curse [b]Item 1391[/b].

217.text: You feel a jolt of electricity from the fence as the chain breaks. Suffer 1 heat damage; you may go north to [b]Location 222[/b].

218.text: With a few adjustments, one of the shards becomes an attractive grenade. Gain item [b]Item 860[/b].

219.text: Without leaving their post, the sentient powers down their shield and raises their metallic fists. Despite their fearsome appearance, they’re not a particularly good fighter, and you soon overcome them and claim their shield as your own. Gain item [b]Item 982[/b].

022.action: [ec1c3b]SMASH[-] the stones around the edges of the array
022.text: The lasers originate far deeper into the rock than you can reach, and your attempts at brute force fall short. Refresh up to 2 challenge dice from your grid and continue.

220.text: Despite the thickness of the ice, you manage to slice through the lorestone and cause it to fall onto its side. Embedded into the bottom half is a red crystal. Gain item [b]Item 1165[/b].

221.text: The beast fights back with reckless abandon, but you overwhelm it. If you pay 1 boost, gain item [b]Item 1160[/b] from the carcass. If not, gain item [b]Item 1190[/b]. Either way, you may go north to [b]Location 602[/b] or pay [2694d0]1 move[-] to go to cave [b]Location 022[/b].

222.text: You feel bad for the pup, but you don’t know how to take care of a creature like this. Plus, you’re hungry and cold. Gain 1 health. If you pay 1 boost, also gain item [b]Item 1164[/b].

223.text: Despite the snow, you manage to cause the lorestone to fall onto its side. Embedded into the bottom half is a yellow crystal. Gain item [b]Item 1164[/b].

224.text: The mountain is so steep that animals share the path with sentients, so you set a basic trap and wait for it to spring. Set 1 challenge die to time, morale, or health to represent the trap (read options below). Then roll 3 other challenge dice; if any of them match the set die, gain the resulting benefit once (if none match, gain [ec1c3b]1 overpower[-] instead). These dice can come from the dice pool or the center; return them afterward.

choices.option: TIME (try to study an animal’s behavior)
choices.outcome: You observe the critter for a little while before setting it free. Gain 1 boost on your character and 1 boost on each metal card.

choices.option: MORALE (try to gain an animal companion)
choices.outcome: The animal is scared but complacent. You will have to work to gain its trust. Gain animal [b]Item 831[/b].

choices.option: HEALTH (try to kill and eat an animal)
choices.outcome: The trap instantly kills the animal. Gain item [b]Item 1217[/b].

225.action: [ec1c3b]BURN[-] a pile of leaves to distract the occupants of the tent
225.text: When the occupants of the tent dash outside to find the source of the commotion, you slip inside the big tent. Go to interior [b]Location 551[/b].

226.text: With nothing better to do, you pluck some pretty flowers. Gain 1 morale and continue.

227.text: The device doesn’t move—it requires a rider. If you pay 1 boost, gain vehicle [b]Item 985[/b]. Otherwise, gain 1 boost and continue.

228.action: [ec1c3b]DUEL[-] the sentient in a card and dice game
228.text: The glowing sentient eagerly reveals their favorite cards and selects one for the duel. Place card [b]Location 798[/b] on the table (it’s a large card) and follow its instructions, using card [b]Item 1519[/b] as the opponent’s card.Variant: Quick Skirm (ignore card 798).

229.text: At the moment when your hands should make contact with the Traveler, instead they meet no resistance, sending you tumbling off the cliff. Lose 3 health and go north to [b]Location 234[/b].

023.text: With a mighty effort, the gate crumbles to the ground. Proceed deeper into the planet to cave [b]Location 083[/b].

230.text: Much to your surprise, the stone is hollow, and there is a key inside. Gain item [b]Item 917[/b].

231.text: The energy in the gauntlet resists the brute force with which you wield it, but once your fingertips reach the end of the glove, it is yours. Gain item [b]Item 1001[/b] and perform no other location actions here.

232.text: You take a lantern from the tree and manage to capture the swirl of light inside. Gain item [b]Item 1187[/b].

233.text: The judges gasp and welcome you to participate in the trial. Continue with the [a84fc0]look-[b]TRANSLATE[/b][-] action.

234.text: You unearth the vegetation, shaking mud from its roots while the sentients watch in horror. Gain flora [b]Item 1054[/b] and curse [b]Item 1395[/b].

235.action: [ec1c3b]DIG[-] into the grassy patch at your feet
235.text: Several meters underground, you feel a sense of satisfaction and strength. Gain 1 health and 1 boost. You may either stop or continue with an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆2-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result only):

choices.outcome: A solid material halts your progress, and you excavate a small box containing an old card. Gain 1 random card from the Skirm spoiler pack (place it in your reserve, where your entire Skirm collection counts as only 1 reserved card. You can use this type of card from your reserve during [ec1c3b]overpower-[b]DUEL[/b][-] actions, which have further instructions). You may either stop or continue with an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆3-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result only):

A rich, dark layer of sediment is prime for deep fungus. Gain item [b]Item 1136[/b]. You may either stop or continue with an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆4-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result):
The earth beneath your feet gives way, sending you tumbling into the depths. Go to cave [b]Location 096[/b].

236.action: [ec1c3b]STUN[-] a sentient as it emerges from the tower
236.text: A translucent pink sentient blinks into existence from one of the lights on the tower. You catch it off guard, slamming it to the ground. They beg for you to let them live, offering a lesson in magic in exchange. Choose one:

choices.outcome: Gain lesson [b]Item 1424[/b].
The allure of the sentient’s life force is too much for you to resist. They blink out of existence, and you lose 1 morale and gain 2 health.

237.text: “Why would you do that?!” asks the sentient. “Now I’m never going to find the sacred mountain.” You can offer to help them find it (gain 1 boost and quest [b]Item 1678[/b]; add it horizontally to your supply) or defiantly grab a torch (item [b]Item 1086[/b]).

238.text: The ethereal guards try to stop you, but you push them aside. Go to city [b]Location 520[/b].

239.text: You stomp up the stairs, threatening to disembowel anyone who stands in your way. A few flashes of pink briefly light up the swamp, and then you find yourself alone in interior [b]Location 565[/b].

024.action: [ec1c3b]BURN[-] a flame to guide you through the darkness
024.text: You create a small flame that briefly illuminates the void before burning out. Choose one, then you may continue with a [a84fc0]look-[b]EXPLORE[/b][-] action: Refresh 1 challenge die from your grid, gain [a84fc0]1 look[-], or add 1 boost to an egg. Also, if you have a wood resource ([b]Item 1180[/b]), you may lose it to gain a bramble torch ([b]Item 1084[/b]).

240.text: The orb is surprisingly durable, but you manage to crack and break it. The sentient tumbles to the ground. “Why would you do that?” they ask. “I’m trying to charge the geolocator!” Gain item [b]Item 1194[/b] and curse [b]Item 1395[/b].

241.text: A weak point in the stone results in a clean break. Gain item [b]Item 1161[/b].

242.text: The energy elemental paces before you with mild curiosity. First, gain curse [b]Item 1395[/b]. Then gain elemental combat cards [b]Item 1549[/b]–[b]Item 1552[/b] and compile them into a cohesive image. Choose one of those cards and continue to perform an action on it.

243.text: You begin to raze the device, and soon you find yourself in a scrap heap of metal and circuitry. The runecaster is now useless, but you’re impressed with how its creators took a naturally occurring phenomenon—runes— and produced a superior synthetic version. Gain 1 boost and your choice of a metal scrap (item [b]Item 1184[/b]) or a circuit board (item [b]Item 1190[/b]).

244.text: The orb is surprisingly durable, but you manage to crack and break it. The sentient inside is speechless—all they can do is wave their hand and curse you for your recklessness. Gain item [b]Item 1194[/b] and curse [b]Item 1395[/b].

245.text: As you attempt to unearth the vegetation, the flower bursts to life, attempting to shock you. Roll 3 skill dice and gain all [2694d0]move[-] results. Lose 1 health unless you pay [2694d0]1 move[-]. You’re caked in mud by the time you put the flower to rest. Gain flora [b]Item 1074[/b].

246.text: You begin to raze the device, and soon you find yourself in a scrap heap of metal and circuitry. The runecaster is now useless, but you’re impressed with how its creators took a naturally occurring phenomenon— runes—and produced a superior synthetic version. Gain 1 boost and your choice of a metal scrap (item [b]Item 1184[/b]) or a circuit board (item [b]Item 1190[/b]).

247.text: The trio of sentients are not warriors, but three against one is never a fair fight. You get the feeling that they haven’t worked together in a long time, and the fight may have actually been good for them as a council. Gain 3 morale and 1 boost.

248.text: The etchings instantly fade when the tree crashes to the ground. It creates a bridge across the river. You may go north to [b]Location 664[/b].

249.text: Wings may not be great weapons, but they do make it difficult to land a blow. As a result, the hunt takes longer than you’d hoped. If you pay 1 boost, gain item [b]Item 1161[/b] from the carcass. If not, gain item [b]Item 1211[/b].

025.text: With a mighty effort, the gate crumbles to the ground. Proceed deeper into the planet to cave [b]Location 085[/b].

250.text: To avoid the spikes surrounding the box, you knock it off the ledge with a well-aimed stone. It clatters to the dry ground. Gain item [b]Item 952[/b].

251.action: [ec1c3b]PLUCK[-] one of the rotating stones from its axis
251.text: The second you put your weight on the stone, it drops into the gaping pit below, bringing you with it. One of the spikes tears into your arm, and you black out from the pain. Lose 1 health and go to cave [b]Location 049[/b].

252.action: [ec1c3b]DUEL[-] the sentient in a card and dice game
252.text: You wisely recognize that the sentient is a skilled warrior in full control of a wide array of winged creatures, so you challenge them in a cardboard duel instead of one of blades and blood. Place card [b]Location 798[/b] on the table (it’s a large card) and follow its instructions, using card [b]Item 1522[/b] as the opponent’s card. If you win, either gain a marrow vulture (animal [b]Item 826[/b]) with 1 boost on it, or gain sentient [b]Item 1331[/b].Variant: Quick Skirm (ignore card 798).

253.action: [ec1c3b]DIG[-] into the pit to unearth the crystal
253.text: Several meters underground, you feel a sense of satisfaction and strength. Gain 1 health and 1 boost. You may either stop or continue with an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆2-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result only):

choices.outcome: A solid material halts your progress, and you excavate an orange crystal (gain item [b]Item 1162[/b]). You may either stop or continue with an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆3-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result only):

In a rich, dark layer of sediment, you discover a weathered tool. Gain item [b]Item 881[/b]. You may either stop or continue with an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆4-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result):
The earth beneath your feet gives way, sending you tumbling into the depths. Go to cave [b]Location 099[/b].

254.text: In the dim light, you use the animal’s tracks to approach it covertly. Go east to [b]Location 443[/b] and choose one:

choices.outcome: Gain 1 random skill and continue.
You quickly dispatch the beast. If you pay 1 boost, gain item [b]Item 1161[/b] from the carcass. If not, gain item [b]Item 1185[/b].

255.text: With well-practiced efficiency, they surround you and attack in unison, each blow leaving you bruised and winded. Finally you prevail, adding a heavy war hammer to your arsenal. Gain item [b]Item 950[/b].

256.text: Several meters underground, you feel a sense of satisfaction and strength. Gain 1 health and 1 boost. You may either stop or continue with an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆2-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result):

choices.outcome: A solid material halts your progress, and you excavate a large crystal. Gain item [b]Item 1161[/b] and curse [b]Item 1392[/b].

257.action: [ec1c3b]DEFEND[-] the trapped sentient from the beasts
257.text: It’s four against one, but you prevail. You release the grateful sentient from the cage. Gain sentient [b]Item 1354[/b].

258.text: You probably should have thought twice about fighting a literal arms dealer. You finally overcome the odds, but unfortunately most of the items the peddler was carrying were broken in the scuffle. Lose 1 health unless you pay 1 boost. Gain item [b]Item 1254[/b]; the peddler begrudgingly highlights your current location (view page 11 in the Book of Vantages; you are in region C).

259.text: The beast is slow and dimwitted, but its thick skin is tough to pierce, and its spikes provide a formidable defense. You finally break through. If you pay 1 boost, gain item [b]Item 1163[/b] from the carcass. If not, gain item [b]Item 1186[/b].

026.text: You ignore the puzzle trap and pummel the barrier, but it re-forms as soon as you shatter it. Refresh up to 2 challenge dice from your grid and continue.

260.text: The beast is somewhat vulnerable without an outer shell, but it still has 8 large tentacles. You ultimately manage to kill it. If you pay 1 boost, gain item [b]Item 1164[/b] from the carcass. If not, gain item [b]Item 1186[/b].

261.action: [ec1c3b]HUNT[-] while the sentients watch (only if you have been welcomed as a participant in this trial; otherwise choose another action)
261.text: The judges unleash a beast that seems just as intent on hunting you as you are intent on hunting it. In the first round, roll 6 skill dice on behalf of the animal and X+1 dice for yourself. You may reroll any number of your dice once per round. For each skill type where one hunter has rolled more than the other, they capture the dice of that skill rolled by the other (e.g., if you roll [ec1c3b]2 overpower[-] and the animal rolls [ec1c3b]1 overpower[-], you capture their [ec1c3b]overpower[-] die). Then proceed to the second round of the hunt with the new dice pools. After the third round, you succeed if you have more dice than the animal; gain item [b]Item 1244[/b], an emblem representative of the sinew sentients’ acceptance of you and your people (also, if you have it, lose the sinew curse). Otherwise, you fail and lose 1 health.

262.text: The burly guards try to stop you, but you push them aside. Gain ability [b]Item 1512[/b] and go to city [b]Location 524[/b].

263.text: As you attempt to unearth the vegetation, the flower bursts to life, the stone-like bulbs pummeling you from all sides. Roll 3 skill dice and gain all [2694d0]move[-] results. Lose 1 health unless you pay [2694d0]1 move[-]. You’re caked in sweat and sand by the time you put the flower to rest. Gain flora [b]Item 1052[/b].

264.text: Several sentients scatter as you plow through the ruins. Through the cloud of red dust, all you can find is a pair of shackles. Gain item [b]Item 937[/b].

265.action: [ec1c3b]SUBDUE[-] the lead sentient until they release their captive
265.text: The sentient is not happy that they’re returning to the bounty hunter’s guild empty handed, but they respect your use of brute force. The captive expresses their gratitude with a gift before heading west by themselves. Gain item [b]Item 874[/b] and 1 boost.

266.action: [ec1c3b]DUEL[-] the sentient in a card and dice game
266.text: The sentient eagerly reveals a vast collection of cards. However, the casino does not take kindly to you gambling outside instead of partaking of their offerings. You’re escorted east by a trio of bouncers. Go to [b]Location 267[/b].

267.action: [ec1c3b]ATTACK[-] the sentient while they’re busy in the oasis
267.text: It’s good that you have the element of surprise, as they fight like a dancer, never striking back, but easily dodging you. It’s difficult to land a blow, but you finally overcome them. Celebrate your victory by drinking the cool oasis water and claiming the sentient’s shovel as your own. Gain item [b]Item 894[/b] and 1 health.

choices.outcome: You drive away the sinew sentient, keeping the leaf sentient relatively unharmed. They thank you with a treasured gift of their people. Gain item [b]Item 1248[/b] (also, if you have it, lose the leaf curse).

You strike the leaf sentient in the jaw, leaving them unconscious. The sinew sentient, finding no pleasure in unresponsive prey, latches onto you in the hopes of better entertainment. Gain sentient [b]Item 1332[/b].

269.action: [ec1c3b]DIG[-] a hole to protect yourself from the storm
269.text: Several meters underground, you feel a sense of satisfaction and strength. Gain 1 health and 1 boost. You may either continue or pay 2 boost to dig deeper:

choices.outcome: A solid material halts your progress, and you excavate an old wooden box with a weathered card inside. Gain 1 random card from the Skirm spoiler pack (place it in your reserve, where your entire Skirm collection counts as only 1 reserved card. You can use this type of card from your reserve during [ec1c3b]overpower-[b]DUEL[/b][-] actions, which have further instructions). You are now safe from the storm; you may pay 1 boost to go in any direction (including north to [b]Location 274[/b] and south to [b]Location 264[/b]).

027.action: [ec1c3b]SMASH[-] the stones around the edges of the array
027.text: The lasers originate far deeper into the rock than you can reach, and your attempts at brute force fall short. Refresh up to 2 challenge dice from your grid and continue.

270.text: After great resistance, the lock snaps open. Roll 5 skill dice; you may reroll each die once. If you have at least 3 of a kind, gain item [b]Item 924[/b]. Instead, if you have at least one pair, gain item [b]Item 1165[/b]. Otherwise, the chest clamps on your leg like a trap; gain 1 boost and (if possible) lose 1 skill.

271.text: Several meters underground, you feel a sense of satisfaction and strength. Gain 1 health and 1 boost. You may either stop or continue with an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆2-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result only):

choices.outcome: A solid material halts your progress, and you excavate a curious box of coins and cards. Gain $5 and 1 random card from the Skirm spoiler pack (place it in your reserve, where your entire Skirm collection counts as only 1 reserved card. You can use this type of card from your reserve during [ec1c3b]overpower-[b]DUEL[/b][-] actions, which have further instructions). You may either stop or continue with an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆3-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result only):

A rich, dark layer of sediment is prime for deep fungus. Gain item [b]Item 1136[/b]. You may either stop or continue with an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆4-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result):
The earth beneath your feet gives way, sending you tumbling into the depths. Go to cave [b]Location 087[/b].

272.text: You’re curious to see what happens to a blob if the stone pillar is no longer underneath it. It doesn’t budge—apparently its levitation is entirely unrelated to the pillar. You find a nice rock among the remains, though. Gain item [b]Item 1179[/b].

273.text: Sparks fly as you carve a large gash in the ship, revealing precious cargo—your power armor. You may replace your character with the corresponding card ([2694d0]captain[-]: [b]Item 1447[/b]; [a84fc0]navigator[-]: [b]Item 1448[/b]; [fd8c46]scholar[-]: [b]Item 1449[/b]; [0b955e]medic[-]: [b]Item 1450[/b]; [fff349]engineer[-]: [b]Item 1451[/b]; [ec1c3b]marine[-]: [b]Item 1452[/b]); transfer all boost from your original character onto the new card. If you already have your power armor, instead gain 3 boost.

274.text: You unearth the vegetation, shaking dirt from the roots. Gain flora [b]Item 1051[/b].

275.action: [ec1c3b]PLUCK[-] the lotus, careful not to damage the roots or petals
275.text: You pluck the lotus, shaking sand from its roots. Gain flora [b]Item 1159[/b].

276.action: [ec1c3b]DUEL[-] the sentients in their dice game
276.text: The sentients eagerly show off their vast collections of cards, and the gold sentient accepts your offer to duel. Place card [b]Location 798[/b] on the table (it’s a large card) and follow its instructions, using card [b]Item 1521[/b] as the opponent’s card.Variant: Quick Skirm (ignore card 798).

277.text: You can’t help but take pity on the creature, chained to the gateway like this. It sighs in relief when you demonstrate what you will do. Its carcass tumbles to the ground. You may either eat it to gain 1 health or bury it to gain 1 morale. If you pay 1 boost, also gain item [b]Item 1161[/b].

278.text: The sand instantly fills even the smallest hole, and you quickly give up. Gain 1 boost and continue.

279.action: [ec1c3b]FORCE[-] the sentient away from the orb
279.text: The sentient glides away, leaving you alone with this strange object. The outside of the orb is a message board. You may read what other travelers have posted about this area and help other wayward travelers by visiting orb.stonemaiergames.com; gain 1 boost.

028.text: You quickly learn that there is a difference between a successful attack and a victorious attack, as not even a single blow budges the sentinel from their post. Refresh up to 2 challenge dice from your grid and continue.

280.text: The sentient has the audacity to continue their conversation with the Traveler as you slash away. Every move they make is a millisecond faster than you. Technically you win the fight when they finally surrender, but it’s really an act of mercy toward you. At least you got a good workout. Gain 1 health.

281.text: You charge inside, head down, fists swinging… only to find yourself alone. Before you get a good look around, someone small and strong smashes into you, knocking you unconscious. Some time later, you awake in a rowdy, underground arena, armed with some arrows. Gain item [b]Item 1235[/b] and go to cave [b]Location 085[/b].

282.text: Several meters underground, you feel a sense of satisfaction and strength. Gain 1 health and 1 boost. You may either stop or continue with an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆2-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result):

choices.outcome: A solid material halts your progress, and you excavate a large crystal. Gain item [b]Item 1163[/b].

283.text: You begin to raze the device, and soon you find yourself in a scrap heap of metal and circuitry. The runecaster is now useless, but you’re impressed with how its creators took a naturally occurring phenomenon—runes— and produced a superior synthetic version. Gain 1 boost and your choice of a metal scrap (item [b]Item 1184[/b]) or a circuit board (item [b]Item 1190[/b]).

284.text: Just in case the crystal is booby trapped, you destroy the entire claw-shaped rock and grab the crystal. Two of the nearby treasure chests are crushed, but the third springs open, a long-fanged guardian slashing at you. Roll 3 skill dice and gain all [ec1c3b]overpower[-] results. Lose 1 health unless you pay [ec1c3b]1 overpower[-]. Gain item [b]Item 1162[/b].

285.text: Sparks fly as you carve a large gash in the ship, revealing precious cargo—your power armor. You may replace your character with the corresponding card ([2694d0]captain[-]: [b]Item 1447[/b]; [a84fc0]navigator[-]: [b]Item 1448[/b]; [fd8c46]scholar[-]: [b]Item 1449[/b]; [0b955e]medic[-]: [b]Item 1450[/b]; [fff349]engineer[-]: [b]Item 1451[/b]; [ec1c3b]marine[-]: [b]Item 1452[/b]); transfer all boost from your original character onto the new card. If you already have your power armor, instead gain 3 boost.

286.text: You expected more of a fight from the 6-armed sentient, but they go limp as soon as you land your first blow. They must be tired from arguing with themself. Freed from their debate for the first time in what must be ages, they surprise you by offering to join your adventure. Gain sentient [b]Item 1342[/b].

287.text: You notice movement in the waning light, so you set a basic trap and wait for it to spring. Set 1 challenge die to time, morale, or health to represent the trap (read options below). Then roll 3 other challenge dice; if any of them match the set die, gain the resulting benefit once (if none match, gain [ec1c3b]1 overpower[-] instead). These dice can come from the dice pool or the center; return them afterward.

choices.option: TIME (try to study an animal’s behavior)
choices.outcome: You observe the critter for a little while before setting it free. Gain 1 boost on your character and 1 boost on each sand card.

choices.option: MORALE (try to gain an animal companion)
choices.outcome: The animal is scared but complacent. You will have to work to gain its trust. Gain animal [b]Item 836[/b].

choices.option: HEALTH (try to kill and eat an animal)
choices.outcome: The trap instantly kills the animal. Gain item [b]Item 1217[/b].

288.text: Backed into a corner, the animal doesn’t stand much of a chance against you, but it fights tooth and nail regardless until you overcome it. If you pay 1 boost, gain item [b]Item 1164[/b] from the carcass. If not, gain item [b]Item 1194[/b].

289.text: To your amazement, at the moment when your hands should have contacted the red-clothed figure, instead they pass through the air as if the Traveler isn’t there at all. Gain 1 boost and continue.

029.action: [ec1c3b]SMASH[-] the stones around the edges of the array
029.text: The lasers originate far deeper into the rock than you can reach, and your attempts at brute force fall short. Refresh up to 2 challenge dice from your grid and continue.

290.text: Several meters underground, you feel a sense of satisfaction and strength. Gain 1 health and 1 boost. You may either stop or continue with an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆2-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result):

choices.outcome: A solid material halts your progress, and you excavate a large crystal. Gain item [b]Item 1162[/b] and continue.

291.action: [ec1c3b]TRAP[-] the beast that lives in this hole
291.text: To your surprise, the hole IS the beast—as you lay the bait, the jagged teeth snap shut, almost tearing your arm off. A massive centipede bursts from the sand, and you hang onto one of its spikes for dear life. Set 1 challenge die to time, morale, or health to represent the trap (read options below). Then roll 3 other challenge dice; if any of them match the set die, gain the resulting benefit once (if none match, gain [ec1c3b]1 overpower[-] instead). These dice can come from the dice pool or the center; return them afterward.

choices.option: TIME (try to study an animal’s behavior)
choices.outcome: You observe the sand centipede for a little while before setting it free. Gain 1 boost on your character and 1 boost on each sand card; also gain item [b]Item 991[/b] after finding it in a glob of the animal’s drool.

choices.option: MORALE (try to gain an animal companion)
choices.outcome: The animal is furious but complacent. You will have to work to gain its trust. Gain animal [b]Item 832[/b].

choices.option: HEALTH (try to kill and eat an animal)
choices.outcome: The trap instantly kills the animal. Gain item [b]Item 1217[/b]; you also gain item [b]Item 991[/b] after finding it lodged in the animal’s fangs.

292.text: Stumbling and scraping against the rough tunnel walls, you crash into a solid metal barrier. You dent it, but it stands firm. Lose 1 health and gain [ec1c3b]1 overpower[-]; go to cave [b]Location 025[/b].

293.text: Roll 3 skill dice up to 3 times (always rolling all dice); roll 1 extra die if you pay a maximum of 1 boost from a food or flora. Based on number of [ec1c3b]overpower[-] in the final results, gain the following:

choices.outcome: You don’t catch anything other than a brief glimpse of fish. Gain 1 boost.
Catch a small but savory fish; gain 1 health.
Catch a medium-sized fish; gain 1 health and 1 boost.
Catch a large fish; gain item [b]Item 1226[/b].
Catch a monstrosity; gain item [b]Item 1232[/b].

294.action: [ec1c3b]SMASH[-] some rocks together to alarm the sleeping sentients
294.text: When the occupants of the tents dash to find the source of the commotion, you slip inside. Go to interior [b]Location 552[/b].

295.text: The sentient barely puts up a fight, but the fiery insect is very protective of their companion. Roll 3 skill dice and gain all [2694d0]move[-] results. Lose 1 health unless you pay [2694d0]1 move[-]. You stumble away from the encounter regretting your choice to attack, but you now have the animal in tow. Gain animal [b]Item 814[/b].

296.text: Despite the sand, you manage to cause the lorestone to fall onto its side. Embedded into the bottom half is a blue crystal. Gain item [b]Item 1160[/b].

297.text: Several meters underground, you feel a sense of satisfaction and strength. Gain 1 health and 1 boost. You may either stop or continue with an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆2-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result):

choices.outcome: The earth beneath your feet gives way, sending you tumbling into the depths. Go to cave [b]Location 025[/b].

298.text: You manage to strike a lorestone, and it spirals gracelessly to the ground. The impact cracks it open, and inside you find a purple crystal. Gain item [b]Item 1161[/b].

299.action: [ec1c3b]STUN[-] a sentient as it emerges from the tower
299.text: A translucent pink sentient blinks into existence from one of the lights on the tower. You catch it off guard, slamming it to the ground. They beg for you to let them live, offering a lesson in magic in exchange. Choose one:

choices.outcome: Gain lesson [b]Item 1424[/b].
The allure of the sentient’s life force is too much for you to resist. They blink out of existence, and you lose 1 morale and gain 2 health.

030.text: You quickly learn that there is a difference between a successful attack and a victorious attack, as not even a single blow budges the sentinel from their post. Refresh up to 2 challenge dice from your grid and continue.

300.text: Acting out of tough love, you use a maneuver that your grandmother taught you on the farm during childhood trips to the country. You pin down the animal’s neck and use its strength to tip over the rest of its body. When you do, you can clearly see the source of its suffering, a large thorn caught in its eye. If you pay [0b955e]2 help[-], you remove the thorn (gain animal [b]Item 841[/b]). If not, gain [0b955e]2 help[-] and continue.

301.text: You land every blow, but it’s like punching maple syrup. The sentient is bewildered by your flailing, and they simply deposit some coins on the cliff as if tipping you for a performance. Gain $3.

302.action: [ec1c3b]TARGET[-] a low-flying bird-like creature as it glides nearby
302.text: Your aim rings true and the bird plummets to the ground nearby. However, the birdkeeper notices your malevolent act and curses you. Gain item [b]Item 1186[/b] and curse [b]Item 1392[/b].

303.action: [ec1c3b]CLEAVE[-] the lorestone into the sea below
303.text: The large stone hits the water with a very satisfying “plunk!” Gain 2 morale.

304.action: [ec1c3b]SUBDUE[-] the only guard you can see
304.text: You bind the distraught guard and make your way into the building, where you only have a few minutes until the pirates arrive. Go to interior [b]Location 566[/b].

305.text: The door is locked, but the hinge easily gives way. Go up the stairs to interior [b]Location 597[/b].

306.text: As you break the shard from its base, the five floating islands move with amazing grace and pummel you one at a time. Roll 3 skill dice and gain all [ec1c3b]overpower[-] results. Lose 1 health unless you pay [ec1c3b]1 overpower[-]. Gain item [b]Item 1216[/b].

307.text: Several meters underground, you feel a sense of satisfaction and strength. Gain 1 health and 1 boost. You may either stop or continue with an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆2-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result):

choices.outcome: A solid material halts your progress, and you excavate a large crystal. Gain item [b]Item 1160[/b] and continue.

308.text: You can’t help but take pity on the creature, and it sighs in relief when you demonstrate what you will do. Its carcass tumbles to the shallows. You may either eat it to gain 1 health or bury it to gain 1 morale. If you pay 1 boost, also gain item [b]Item 1162[/b].

309.action: [ec1c3b]TARGET[-] either the ship or the flying sentient

choices.outcome: Your aim rings true, puncturing the sails, but the ship stays afloat. Gain 1 boost and gain [2694d0]3 move[-]; continue.

Your aim rings true, and the sentient floats to the shore like a leaf on the wind. The ship instantly loses its buoyancy and crashes into the shallows, the hull splintering. A package drifts over to you. Gain item [b]Item 1034[/b].

031.text: You quickly learn that there is a difference between a successful attack and a victorious attack, as not even a single blow budges the sentinel from their post. Refresh up to 2 challenge dice from your grid and continue.

310.action: [ec1c3b]DIG[-] into the grassy patch at the end of the rainbow
310.text: Several meters underground, you feel a sense of satisfaction and strength. Gain 1 health and 1 boost. You may either stop or continue with an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆2-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result only):

choices.outcome: A solid material halts your progress, and you excavate a lost purse full of coins. Gain $10. You may either stop or continue with an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆3-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result only):

A rich, dark layer of sediment is prime for deep fungus. Gain item [b]Item 1136[/b]. You may either stop or continue with an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆4-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result):
The earth beneath your feet gives way, sending you tumbling into the depths. Go to cave [b]Location 089[/b].

311.text: Your tactics are a success. Gain item [b]Item 1182[/b] from the feathery carcass. If you pay 1 boost, also gain item [b]Item 1161[/b].

312.text: Roll 3 skill dice up to 3 times (always rolling all dice); roll 1 extra die if you pay a maximum of 1 boost from a food or flora. Based on number of [ec1c3b]overpower[-] in the final results, gain the following:

choices.outcome: You don’t catch anything other than a brief glimpse of fish. Gain 1 boost.
Catch a small but savory fish; gain 1 health.
Catch a medium-sized fish; gain 1 health and 1 boost.
Catch a large fish; gain item [b]Item 1226[/b].
Catch a monstrosity; gain item [b]Item 1232[/b].

313.text: The bird is much more comfortable sparring in the water than you, but you overwhelm it. If you pay 1 boost, gain item [b]Item 1161[/b] from the carcass. If not, gain item [b]Item 1182[/b].
