314.text: Roll 3 skill dice up to 3 times (always rolling all dice); roll 1 extra die if you pay a maximum of 1 boost from a food or flora. Based on number of [ec1c3b]overpower[-] in the final results, gain the following:

choices.outcome: You don’t catch anything other than a brief glimpse of fish. Gain 1 boost.
Catch a small but savory fish; gain 1 health.
Catch a medium-sized fish; gain 1 health and 1 boost.
Catch a large fish; gain item [b]Item 1226[/b].
Catch a monstrosity; gain item [b]Item 1232[/b].

315.text: You unearth the vegetation, shaking dirt from its roots. Gain flora [b]Item 1072[/b].

316.text: This animal uses charm and camouflage as defense tactics, neither of which work well in close combat. If you pay 1 boost, gain item [b]Item 1162[/b] from the carcass. If not, gain item [b]Item 1136[/b].

317.text: You put yourself between the stick-like animal and the large lizard with stone-scaled skin. It lashes out as if to see if you will stand your ground; when you do, it slinks away into the brush. Gain animal [b]Item 851[/b].

318.text: The artifact’s protectors don’t back down from the incursion, and you must fight your way through them. Roll 3 skill dice and gain all [2694d0]move[-] results. Lose 1 health unless you pay [2694d0]1 move[-]. When the raid is over, if you pay 3 boost, you discover a way to activate the artifact (gain item [b]Item 1008[/b] and perform no other location actions here). If not, the mystery of the artifact remains unsolved, and it vanishes from your grasp; gain 1 boost.

319.text: The tiny creature’s thick hide provides only temporary protection against your attack, and you soon pierce it. If you pay 1 boost, gain item [b]Item 1163[/b] from the carcass. If not, gain item [b]Item 1185[/b].

032.text: With a mighty effort, the gate dislodges from the barrier. Proceed deeper into the planet to cave [b]Location 092[/b].

320.text: You unearth the vegetation, shaking dirt from its roots. Choose the flower to pluck; either way, a nearby sentient sees what you’ve done and smites you:

choices.option: The fern-like flower on the left
choices.outcome: Gain flora [b]Item 1072[/b] and curse [b]Item 1388[/b].
Gain flora [b]Item 1067[/b] and curse [b]Item 1388[/b].

321.text: Before you can do any harm, you feel yourself go limp. When you come to your senses, you find that you’ve been transported to location [b]Location 610[/b].

322.action: [ec1c3b]DUEL[-] the sentient in a card and dice game
322.text: You challenge the sentient to a card and dice game. Place card [b]Location 798[/b] on the table (it’s a large card) and follow its instructions, using card [b]Item 1528[/b] as the opponent’s card. If you win, gain flora [b]Item 1071[/b].Variant: Quick Skirm (ignore card 798).

323.action: [ec1c3b]CUT[-] one of the poles holding the tent
323.text: At the sound of the pole snapping, the instructor makes an almost imperceptible motion, and the wood snaps back together. A few students roll their eyes at you as the teacher proceeds with their lesson. Gain 1 boost.

324.text: You can’t help but take pity on the creature, and it sighs in relief when you demonstrate what you will do. The animal perishes in your arms and fades away, leaving only an orange crystal in its wake. Gain item [b]Item 1162[/b].

325.text: You slash through the web, freeing the pup. Gain animal [b]Item 817[/b].

326.text: The creature’s main defense mechanism is its web, which is hard to avoid without stepping into the molten river, but you managed to strike down the animal. If you pay 1 boost, gain item [b]Item 1164[/b] from the carcass. If not, gain item [b]Item 1185[/b].

327.text: After great resistance, the lock snaps open. Roll 5 skill dice; you may reroll each die once. If you have at least 3 of a kind, gain item [b]Item 1039[/b]. Instead, if you have at least one pair, gain $10. Otherwise, if you haven’t already disarmed the chest, the chest pops into your lower jaw; you’re more embarrassed than hurt (lose 1 morale).

328.action: [ec1c3b]DUEL[-] the sentient in a card and dice game
328.text: The gold sentient eagerly reveals a vast collection of cards. Place card [b]Location 798[/b] on the table (it’s a large card) and follow its instructions, using card [b]Item 1521[/b] as the opponent’s card.Variant: Quick Skirm (ignore card 798).

329.text: While the sentient doesn’t have the energy to put up much of a struggle, they have a significant size advantage over you. Finally they collapse and fall to a heap, the light fading from their eyes. You can rob them (gain $8) or take pity on them by nursing them back to health (gain sentient [b]Item 1313[/b] with only 1 boost on them).

033.action: [ec1c3b]BREAK[-] panels off the wall to try to form a crude bridge across the gap
033.text: Your attempts at brute-force bridge building are fruitless. Refresh up to 2 challenge dice from your grid and continue.

330.text: You begin to raze the device, and soon you find yourself in a scrap heap of metal and circuitry. The runecaster is now useless, but you’re impressed with how its creators took a naturally occurring phenomenon—runes— and produced a superior synthetic version. Gain 1 boost and your choice of a metal scrap (item [b]Item 1184[/b]) or a circuit board (item [b]Item 1190[/b]).

331.text: You uproot the tree, golden leaves falling all around you. Nearby sentients are furious that you would remove their sacred Halcyon Shrub. Gain flora [b]Item 1070[/b] and curse [b]Item 1393[/b].

332.text: The guards try to stop you, but you push them aside. Go to city [b]Location 530[/b].

333.text: In the ensuing struggle, many of the more fragile items break. All that remains is a vented jacket. Gain item [b]Item 967[/b].

334.action: [ec1c3b]DUEL[-] the sentient in their favorite game
334.text: Despite their weakened state, the sentient can’t resist their favorite game. Place card [b]Location 798[/b] on the table (it’s a large card) and follow its instructions, using card [b]Item 1521[/b] as the opponent’s card. When the game ends, the sentient thanks you for bringing joy to them in their final moments, and they take their last breath in peace. Gain 1 morale.Variant: Quick Skirm (ignore card 798).

335.text: The peddlers are nonplussed by your grand entrance, and rather than fight back, they offer you a compass and some coins. Choose one:

choices.outcome: Gain item [b]Item 968[/b] and $4.

The peddlers are heavily armed and well-practiced in fending off bandits. They’re also a little peeved that you didn’t accept their offer. When the dust clears, you claim your prize and leave. Gain items [b]Item 896[/b], [b]Item 961[/b], and [b]Item 968[/b].

336.action: [ec1c3b]TARGET[-] the caravan from across the gap
336.text: After realizing they’re being attacked from range, the caravan adopts a defensive formation, the exterior protected by large husks. Gain 1 boost and 1 random skill.

337.text: You attack the vehicle with such ferocity that its occupants have no choice but to surrender their wares, lest you drive them off the cliff. Gain $25 and curse [b]Item 1393[/b].

338.action: [ec1c3b]DUEL[-] a passing sentient in a card and dice game
338.text: The sentient eagerly reveals a vast collection of cards. Place card [b]Location 798[/b] on the table (it’s a large card) and follow its instructions. If you win, they invite you inside; go to interior [b]Location 570[/b].Variant: Quick Skirm (ignore card 798).

339.text: The sentients don’t back down from the incursion, and you must fight your way through them. When the raid is over, if you pay 3 boost, you discover a way to activate the artifact (gain item [b]Item 999[/b] and perform no other location actions here). If not, the mystery of the artifact remains unsolved, and it vanishes from your grasp; gain 1 boost.

034.text: You quickly learn that there is a difference between a successful attack and a victorious attack, as not even a single blow budges the sentinel from their post. Refresh up to 2 challenge dice from your grid and continue.

340.action: [ec1c3b]FORCE[-] the boat free from the mud
340.text: In gratitude for your muscles, the owner of the boat gives you a big hug. They explain that they lost track of time refilling the nearby treasure chest, and their boat got stuck in the mud at low tide. They offer you your choice of a weapon from the treasure chest (gain item [b]Item 1151[/b]) or a calming quest westward to the horn rocks (gain [2694d0]1 move[-] and quest [b]Item 1676[/b]; add it horizontally to your supply).

341.text: The judges gasp and welcome you to participate in the trial. Continue with the [fd8c46]engage-[b]TRADE[/b][-] action.

342.action: [ec1c3b]FORCE[-] a ferry operator to take you to the city
342.text: The ferryman trembles in fear as you command them toward the city. Go to city [b]Location 529[/b].

343.text: You notice movement in the brush, so you set a basic trap and wait for it to spring. Set 1 challenge die to time, morale, or health to represent the trap (read options below). Then roll 3 other challenge dice; if any of them match the set die, gain the resulting benefit once (if none match, gain [ec1c3b]1 overpower[-] instead). These dice can come from the dice pool or the center; return them afterward.

choices.option: TIME (try to study an animal’s behavior)
choices.outcome: You observe the critter for a little while before setting it free. Gain 1 boost on your character and 1 boost on each gold card.

choices.option: MORALE (try to gain an animal companion)
choices.outcome: The animal is scared but complacent. You will have to work to gain its trust. Gain animal [b]Item 817[/b].

choices.option: HEALTH (try to kill and eat an animal)
choices.outcome: The trap instantly kills the animal. Gain item [b]Item 1217[/b].

344.text: Roll 3 skill dice up to 3 times (always rolling all dice); roll 1 extra die if you pay a maximum of 1 boost from a food or flora. Based on number of [ec1c3b]overpower[-] in the final results, gain the following:

choices.outcome: You don’t catch anything other than a brief glimpse of fish. Gain 1 boost.
Catch a small but savory fish; gain 1 health.
Catch a medium-sized fish; gain 1 health and 1 boost.
Catch a large fish; gain item [b]Item 1226[/b].
Catch a monstrosity; gain item [b]Item 1232[/b].

345.action: [ec1c3b]TARGET[-] either the balloon or the beast

choices.outcome: Your projectile pierces the thin material of the balloon, sending it careening off into a distant sand dune. You find the animal on the ground, shaken and with a nasty scrape along its jaw but otherwise unhurt. Gain animal [b]Item 836[/b].

You can’t help but take pity on the injured creature, and your projectile ends its misery. It slumps to the ground. Gain 2 health from the fresh meat. If you pay 1 boost, also gain item [b]Item 1161[/b].

346.action: [ec1c3b]FORCE[-] open the front door, which is heavily fortified, to the point that you wonder if anyone uses it
346.text: With brute violence you force the metal door from its hinges and its several locking mechanisms. Go to interior [b]Location 076[/b].

347.action: [ec1c3b]PLUCK[-] the lotus, careful not to damage the roots or petals
347.text: You pluck the sacred flower. Gain flora [b]Item 1159[/b] and curse [b]Item 1387[/b].

348.text: You begin to raze the device, and soon you find yourself in a scrap heap of metal and circuitry. The runecaster is now useless, but you’re impressed with how its creators took a naturally occurring phenomenon— runes—and produced a superior synthetic version. Gain 1 boost and your choice of a metal scrap (item [b]Item 1184[/b]) or a circuit board (item [b]Item 1190[/b]).

349.action: [ec1c3b]CLEAVE[-] the tower so part of it collapses over the sand dune
349.text: The horizontal tower provides an easy route across the sand. You may go north to [b]Location 179[/b].

035.text: You ignore the puzzle trap and pummel the barrier, but it seals tight as soon as you shatter it. Refresh up to 2 challenge dice from your grid and continue.

350.text: Stumbling and scraping against the rough tunnel walls, you fall into the path of a red-hot laser. Lose 1 health and gain [0b955e]2 help[-]; go to cave [b]Location 029[/b].

351.text: With a satisfying click, the lock pops open. Roll 4 skill dice; you may reroll each die once. If you have at least 3 of a kind, gain item [b]Item 975[/b]. Instead, if you have at least one pair, gain item [b]Item 1161[/b]. Otherwise, gain $4.

352.text: Roll 3 skill dice up to 3 times (always rolling all dice); roll 1 extra die if you pay a maximum of 1 boost from a food or flora. Based on number of [ec1c3b]overpower[-] in the final results, gain the following:

choices.outcome: You don’t catch anything other than a brief glimpse of fish. Gain 1 boost.
Catch a small but savory fish; gain 1 health.
Catch a medium-sized fish; gain 1 health and 1 boost.
Catch a large fish; gain item [b]Item 1226[/b].
Catch a monstrosity; gain item [b]Item 1232[/b].

353.text: Wood sentients in the area cheer as the tower crashes to the ground, though the circuit sentients aren’t happy about it. Gain item [b]Item 1250[/b] and curse [b]Item 1396[/b] (also, if you have it, lose the wood curse).

354.text: You unearth the vegetation, shaking dirt from the roots. Gain flora [b]Item 1050[/b].

355.text: Sparks fly as you carve a large gash in the ship, revealing precious cargo—your power armor. You may replace your character with the corresponding card ([2694d0]captain[-]: [b]Item 1447[/b]; [a84fc0]navigator[-]: [b]Item 1448[/b]; [fd8c46]scholar[-]: [b]Item 1449[/b]; [0b955e]medic[-]: [b]Item 1450[/b]; [fff349]engineer[-]: [b]Item 1451[/b]; [ec1c3b]marine[-]: [b]Item 1452[/b]); transfer all boost from your original character onto the new card. If you already have your power armor, instead gain 3 boost.

356.text: You toss a barnacle-laden stone into the hole, which closes around it and disappears beneath the water. You have temporarily neutralized the dangerous sea centipede. Gain 1 morale and [2694d0]1 move[-]; continue.

357.action: [ec1c3b]DEFEND[-] the residents from the flamethrowing beast
357.text: You knock the glowing insect from the sky, bashing it against the rocks. The sentients inside the house are furious that you would disrupt a waterfighter from doing its job of keeping their house aflame. Gain item [b]Item 1182[/b] from the carcass. If you pay 1 boost, also gain item [b]Item 1163[/b].

358.text: The sentient leaps over to your side of the canal to accept your challenge. The fight is a blur of metal, blades, and flame, and somehow you walk away victorious. The leader graciously offers you knowledge of fire manipulation as your prize. Gain spell [b]Item 1383[/b].

359.text: Roll 3 skill dice up to 3 times (always rolling all dice); roll 1 extra die if you pay a maximum of 1 boost from a food or flora. Based on number of [ec1c3b]overpower[-] in the final results, gain the following:

choices.outcome: You don’t catch anything other than a brief glimpse of fish. Gain 1 boost.
Catch a small but savory fish; gain 1 health.
Catch a medium-sized fish; gain 1 health and 1 boost.
Catch a large fish; gain item [b]Item 1226[/b].
Catch a monstrosity; gain item [b]Item 1232[/b].

036.action: [ec1c3b]BURN[-] a flame to guide you through the darkness
036.text: You create a small flame that briefly illuminates the void before burning out. Choose one, then you may continue with a [a84fc0]look-[b]EXPLORE[/b][-] action: Refresh 1 challenge die from your grid, gain [a84fc0]1 look[-], or add 1 boost to an egg. Also, if you have a wood resource ([b]Item 1180[/b]), you may lose it to gain a bramble torch ([b]Item 1084[/b]).

360.text: Use the rock to create a crude weapon. Gain item [b]Item 872[/b].

361.text: You begin to raze the device, and soon you find yourself in a scrap heap of metal and circuitry. The runecaster is now useless, but you’re impressed with how its creators took a naturally occurring phenomenon—runes— and produced a superior synthetic version. Gain 1 boost and your choice of a metal scrap (item [b]Item 1184[/b]) or a circuit board (item [b]Item 1190[/b]).

362.text: You unearth the vegetation, shaking water and sand from the roots. Gain flora [b]Item 1071[/b].

363.text: Your aim rings true, and the bird-like creature splashes down into the water nearby. To your relief, you find it quite tasty. Gain 1 health and continue.

364.text: You begin to raze the device, and soon you find yourself in a scrap heap of metal and circuitry. The runecaster is now useless, but you’re impressed with how its creators took a naturally occurring phenomenon— runes—and produced a superior synthetic version. Gain 1 boost and your choice of a metal scrap (item [b]Item 1184[/b]) or a circuit board (item [b]Item 1190[/b]).

365.text: The sea is angry this day! You stand firm against the waves, but they continue to roil and churn around you. The best you can muster is a moral victory. Gain 2 morale.

366.text: Roll 3 skill dice up to 3 times (always rolling all dice); roll 1 extra die if you pay a maximum of 1 boost from a food or flora. Based on number of [ec1c3b]overpower[-] in the final results, gain the following:

choices.outcome: You don’t catch anything other than a brief glimpse of fish. Gain 1 boost.
Catch a small but savory fish; gain 1 health.
Catch a medium-sized fish; gain 1 health and 1 boost.
Catch a large fish; gain item [b]Item 1226[/b].
Catch a monstrosity; gain item [b]Item 1232[/b].

367.text: After great resistance, the lock snaps open. Roll 5 skill dice; you may reroll each die once. If you have at least 3 of a kind, gain item [b]Item 924[/b]. Instead, if you have at least one pair, gain item [b]Item 1164[/b]. Otherwise, gain $4.

368.text: Roll 3 skill dice up to 3 times (always rolling all dice); roll 1 extra die if you pay a maximum of 1 boost from a food or flora. Based on number of [ec1c3b]overpower[-] in the final results, gain the following:

choices.outcome: You don’t catch anything other than a brief glimpse of fish. Gain 1 boost.
Catch a small but savory fish; gain 1 health.
Catch a medium-sized fish; gain 1 health and 1 boost.
Catch a large fish; gain item [b]Item 1226[/b].
Catch a monstrosity; gain item [b]Item 1232[/b].

369.text: The wood elemental paces before you with mild curiosity. First, gain curse [b]Item 1386[/b]. Then find elemental combat cards [b]Item 1569[/b]–[b]Item 1572[/b] and compile them into a cohesive image. Choose one of those cards and continue to perform an action on it.

037.text: With a mighty effort, the gate crumbles to the ground. Proceed deeper into the planet to cave [b]Location 097[/b].

370.text: Roll 3 skill dice up to 3 times (always rolling all dice); roll 1 extra die if you pay a maximum of 1 boost from a food or flora. Based on number of [ec1c3b]overpower[-] in the final results, gain the following:

choices.outcome: You don’t catch anything other than a brief glimpse of fish. Gain 1 boost.
Catch a small but savory fish; gain 1 health.
Catch a medium-sized fish; gain 1 health and 1 boost.
Catch a large fish; gain item [b]Item 1226[/b].
Catch a monstrosity; gain item [b]Item 1232[/b].

371.text: You unearth the vegetation, shaking water from the roots. A nearby sentient sees what you’ve done and smites you. Gain flora [b]Item 1049[/b] and curse [b]Item 1395[/b].

372.action: [ec1c3b]SLAY[-] any monster that lurks beneath the depths of the creek
372.text: There is a beast that plagues these waters, and you drag it to the shore and dispatch it with vigor. Sentients in the area cheer and give you a pair of flippers, saying you may need them to the north. Gain item [b]Item 927[/b] and 1 morale; you may go north to [b]Location 302[/b].

373.text: The beast bellows in anger as you attack, but its rage leaves it vulnerable to your first strike. If you pay 1 boost, gain item [b]Item 1163[/b] from the carcass. If not, gain item [b]Item 1181[/b].

374.text: The beast is agile, well-shielded, and full of sharp bits. Gain item [b]Item 1179[/b] from the carcass. If you pay 1 boost, also gain item [b]Item 1160[/b].

375.text: Two people are aboard the ship—a young couple out for an impromptu overnight voyage. All they have to offer is a fancy toothbrush, which they suggest you use as soon as possible. Gain item [b]Item 1208[/b] and $2.

376.action: [ec1c3b]SUBDUE[-] the owner of the building while you help yourself to their belongings
376.text: The owner curses you as you enter their workshop. Gain curse [b]Item 1394[/b] and go to interior [b]Location 571[/b].

377.text: You unearth the vegetation, shaking dirt from the roots. The sentients to the east see what you’ve done and smite you. Gain flora [b]Item 1069[/b] and curse [b]Item 1394[/b].

378.action: [ec1c3b]TARGET[-] a sentient with a well-timed throw
378.text: The other merchants laugh at their competitor’s misfortune and toss you a few coins in appreciation for the entertainment. Gain $5 and continue; if you later attempt to gain an item from these merchants, the map is not an available option.

379.text: Roll 3 skill dice up to 3 times (always rolling all dice); roll 1 extra die if you pay a maximum of 1 boost from a food or flora. Based on number of [ec1c3b]overpower[-] in the final results, gain the following:

choices.outcome: You don’t catch anything other than a brief glimpse of fish. Gain 1 boost.
Catch a small but savory fish; gain 1 health.
Catch a medium-sized fish; gain 1 health and 1 boost.
Catch a large fish; gain item [b]Item 1226[/b].
Catch a monstrosity; gain item [b]Item 1232[/b].

038.text: You ignore the puzzle trap and pummel the barrier, but it grows back as soon as you shatter it. Refresh up to 2 challenge dice from your grid and continue.

380.text: You can’t help but take pity on the creature, and it sighs in relief when you demonstrate what you will do. You make a gentle incision just above its eyes, and its carcass tumbles to the ground. You may either eat it to gain 1 health or bury it to gain 1 morale. If you pay 1 boost, also gain item [b]Item 1162[/b].

381.text: Facing off with the giant turtle, you attack, slashing at its webbed feet. After putting up a fight, the turtle collapses into the ocean floor. If you pay 1 boost, gain item [b]Item 1186[/b] from the carcass. You may also pay 1 boost to gain item [b]Item 1160[/b].

382.text: As the boulder rolls over the bushes, you hear a loud clank. The boulder has come to rest on top of a sword. Gain 1 boost. You may choose one of the following to reach the sword:

choices.outcome: Gain item [b]Item 889[/b].
Gain item [b]Item 889[/b].

383.action: [ec1c3b]DUEL[-] the sentient in a card and dice game
383.text: The sentient’s eyes light up at the prospect of playing their favorite game. Place card [b]Location 798[/b] on the table (it’s a large card) and follow its instructions, using card [b]Item 1528[/b] as the opponent’s card.Variant: Quick Skirm (ignore card 798).

384.text: After great resistance, the lock snaps open. Roll 5 skill dice; you may reroll each die once. If you have at least 3 of a kind, gain item [b]Item 1214[/b]. Instead, if you have at least one pair, gain item [b]Item 1165[/b]. Otherwise, the chest clamps onto your fingers and won’t let go (end your turn).

385.text: The stone crashes to the ground, much to the dismay of sentients in the area. Despite the disrespectful act, you’re happy to see an axe tumble out of the rubble. Gain item [b]Item 872[/b] and curse [b]Item 1386[/b].

386.action: [ec1c3b]PLUCK[-] the lotus, careful not to damage the roots or petals
386.text: You pluck the fragrantly perfumed flower. Gain flora [b]Item 1159[/b].

387.text: You break the peace of this valley by hunting one of the hulking creatures. The others come to its aid, and you’re soon fending off beasts from all directions. Gain item [b]Item 1181[/b]. If you pay 1 boost, also gain item [b]Item 1163[/b].

388.text: You rush down the hill, catching the guards unaware. With a few swift maneuvers you hogtie them in the brush, waving other travelers onto the bridge free of charge. Gain 1 morale; you may go west to [b]Location 780[/b].

389.text: You unearth the vegetation, shaking dirt from the roots. A sentient to the east sees what you’ve done and smites you. Gain flora [b]Item 1068[/b] and curse [b]Item 1389[/b].

039.action: [ec1c3b]BURN[-] a flame to guide you through the darkness
039.text: You create a small flame that briefly illuminates the void before burning out. Choose one, then you may continue with a [a84fc0]look-[b]EXPLORE[/b][-] action: Refresh 1 challenge die from your grid, gain [a84fc0]1 look[-], or add 1 boost to an egg. Also, if you have a wood resource ([b]Item 1180[/b]), you may lose it to gain a bramble torch ([b]Item 1084[/b]).

390.text: The Traveler was right—the berries are delicious! Gain 1 morale and continue.

391.text: Several meters underground, you feel a sense of satisfaction and strength. Gain 1 health and 1 boost. You may either stop or continue with an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆2-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result only):

choices.outcome: Now past the wall, you may break free on the other side. If you do, sentients scatter as you emerge from the ground into a busy square. Gain $5 from a dropped purse. You may either go west to [b]Location 788[/b] or perform an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆3-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result only):

A rich, dark layer of sediment is prime for deep fungus. Gain item [b]Item 1136[/b]. You may either stop or continue with an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆4-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result):
The earth beneath your feet gives way, sending you tumbling into the depths. Go to cave [b]Location 079[/b].

392.action: [ec1c3b]PLUCK[-] some berries and squish them between your fingers before tasting one that looks ripe
392.text: Roll 3 skill dice up to 3 times. If you roll at least [0b955e]1 help[-], gain 2 health. Otherwise lose 1 health.

393.text: The metal elemental paces before you with mild curiosity. First, gain curse [b]Item 1391[/b]. Then find elemental combat cards [b]Item 1573[/b]–[b]Item 1576[/b] and compile them into a cohesive image. Choose one of those cards and continue to perform an action on it.

394.text: Sparks fly as you carve a large gash in the ship, revealing precious cargo—your power armor. You may replace your character with the corresponding card ([2694d0]captain[-]: [b]Item 1447[/b]; [a84fc0]navigator[-]: [b]Item 1448[/b]; [fd8c46]scholar[-]: [b]Item 1449[/b]; [0b955e]medic[-]: [b]Item 1450[/b]; [fff349]engineer[-]: [b]Item 1451[/b]; [ec1c3b]marine[-]: [b]Item 1452[/b]); transfer all boost from your original character onto the new card. If you already have your power armor, instead gain 3 boost.

395.action: [ec1c3b]SMASH[-] the base of one of the objects
395.text: Given the polarity of these two huge magnets, as soon as you loosen one from its base, it’s propelled away with such force that it takes out a chunk of the mountain, clearing an easy path north. Gain 2 morale; from now on the cost to move north from here is 1.

396.text: The observatory door yields after a few kicks. Go to interior [b]Location 572[/b].

397.action: [ec1c3b]PLUCK[-] the lotus, careful not to damage the roots or petals
397.text: You pluck the pungent flower. Gain flora [b]Item 1159[/b] and curse [b]Item 1387[/b].

398.action: [ec1c3b]DIG[-] into the grassy patch at your feet
398.text: Several meters underground, you feel a sense of satisfaction and strength. Gain 1 health and 1 boost. You may either stop or continue with an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆2-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result only):

choices.outcome: A solid material halts your progress, and you excavate a lost purse full of coins. Gain $10. You may either stop or continue with an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆3-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result only):

A rich, dark layer of sediment is prime for deep fungus. Gain item [b]Item 1136[/b]. You may either stop or continue with an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆4-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result):
The earth beneath your feet gives way, sending you tumbling into the depths. Go to cave [b]Location 082[/b].

399.text: Roll 3 skill dice up to 3 times (always rolling all dice); roll 1 extra die if you pay a maximum of 1 boost from a food or flora. Based on number of [ec1c3b]overpower[-] in the final results, gain the following:

choices.outcome: You don’t catch anything other than a brief glimpse of fish. Gain 1 boost.
Catch a small but savory fish; gain 1 health.
Catch a medium-sized fish; gain 1 health and 1 boost.
Catch a large fish; gain item [b]Item 1226[/b].
Catch a monstrosity; gain item [b]Item 1232[/b].

040.action: [ec1c3b]FORCE[-] the artifact free of its axis
040.text: You reach out to the crystal, and it snaps off into your hands. You blink, and when your eyes open, you’re back on the surface with the crystal in your hands. Gain item [b]Item 1000[/b] and go to [b]Location 157[/b]; if you have a previous location card for reference, return it to the box.

400.text: You feel the satisfaction of moving soil from one place to another. Before you know it, you’ve created a tunnel all the way down to the base of the cliff. Gain 1 health and go north to [b]Location 401[/b].

401.text: After the devices crash to the ground, you look around, half expecting to be swarmed by angry citizens. Instead, you hear the sounds of elation from several glowing sentients. They explain that the circuit sentients use these towers as range extenders, allowing them to travel farther from their homeland… and invasively into areas like this swamp. The sentients offer you some tips for moving through this biome. Gain ability [b]Item 1489[/b].

402.text: The remnant of the felled tree acts as a battering ram as you force your way through a weak spot in the wall. Gain 1 morale and go east to [b]Location 621[/b].

403.action: [ec1c3b]CUT[-] down a tree so that it falls into the fence
403.text: Thanks to the bitter cold, it doesn’t take much to send the tree crashing down. It flattens the fence and sends sparks flying. Before anyone notices, you may go east to [b]Location 626[/b].

404.text: The old stone crashes to the ground, and you’re happy to see that a carabiner detached from the column. Gain item [b]Item 890[/b].

405.text: You begin to raze the device, and soon you find yourself in a scrap heap of metal and circuitry. The runecaster is now useless, but you’re impressed with how its creators took a naturally occurring phenomenon— runes—and produced a superior synthetic version. Gain 1 boost and your choice of a metal scrap (item [b]Item 1184[/b]) or a circuit board (item [b]Item 1190[/b]).

406.text: The pest attempts to evade you, but there’s nowhere to go on the tiny island, and you hunt it down. If you pay 1 boost, gain item [b]Item 1164[/b] from the carcass. If not, gain item [b]Item 1194[/b].

407.action: [ec1c3b]STUN[-] a sentient as it emerges from the tower
407.text: A translucent pink sentient blinks into existence from one of the lights on the tower. You catch it off guard, slamming it to the ground. They beg for you to let them live, offering a lesson in magic in exchange. Choose one:

choices.outcome: Gain lesson [b]Item 1424[/b].
The allure of the sentient’s life force is too much for you to resist. They blink out of existence, and you lose 1 morale and gain 2 health.

408.action: [ec1c3b]CLEAVE[-] into the cliffside building from above
408.text: The soft seaside dirt opens into a room filled with all sorts of odds and ends. Go to interior [b]Location 559[/b].

409.text: You allow the power of the spires to seep into you. Gain spell [b]Item 1371[/b].

041.text: The door to a surveillance center is ajar. Choose one:

choices.outcome: Go to interior [b]Location 042[/b].

choices.option: Duel a nearby sentient in a card and dice game
choices.outcome: The sentient’s red eyes light up at the prospect of playing their favorite game, and they promise not to cheat much. Place card [b]Location 798[/b] on the table (it’s a large card) and follow its instructions, using card [b]Item 1523[/b] as the opponent’s card.Variant: Quick Skirm (ignore card 798).

410.text: The result of you entering the building is based on the current occupancy status. Proceed if you already know this status; if not, roll a challenge die to randomly determine the status:

choices.option: No one is home (blank challenge die)
choices.outcome: Go to interior [b]Location 573[/b]; on your next turn, you may not choose the [fd8c46]engage-[b]DEFUSE[/b][-] action there.

The house is occupied. Your forceful entrance sends the family running (gain curse [b]Item 1389[/b] and go to interior [b]Location 573[/b]; on your next turn, you may not choose the [fd8c46]engage-[b]DEFUSE[/b][-] action there.).

411.text: You notice movement in the brush, so you set a basic trap and wait for it to spring. Set 1 challenge die to time, morale, or health to represent the trap (read options below). Then roll 3 other challenge dice; if any of them match the set die, gain the resulting benefit once (if none match, gain [ec1c3b]1 overpower[-] instead). These dice can come from the dice pool or the center; return them afterward.

choices.option: TIME (try to study an animal’s behavior)
choices.outcome: You observe the critter for a little while before setting it free. Gain 1 boost on your character and 1 boost on each stone card.

choices.option: MORALE (try to gain an animal companion)
choices.outcome: The animal is scared but complacent. You will have to work to gain its trust. Gain animal [b]Item 838[/b].

choices.option: HEALTH (try to kill and eat an animal)
choices.outcome: The trap instantly kills the animal. Gain item [b]Item 1217[/b].

412.text: Roll 3 skill dice up to 3 times (always rolling all dice); roll 1 extra die if you pay a maximum of 1 boost from a food or flora. Based on number of [ec1c3b]overpower[-] in the final results, gain the following:

choices.outcome: You don’t catch anything other than a brief glimpse of fish. Gain 1 boost.
Catch a small but savory fish; gain 1 health.
Catch a medium-sized fish; gain 1 health and 1 boost.
Catch a large fish; gain item [b]Item 1226[/b].
Catch a monstrosity; gain item [b]Item 1232[/b].

413.action: [ec1c3b]PLUCK[-] the lotus, careful not to damage the roots or petals
413.text: You pluck the majestic flower. Gain flora [b]Item 1159[/b].

414.text: Roll 3 skill dice up to 3 times (always rolling all dice); roll 1 extra die if you pay a maximum of 1 boost from a food or flora. Based on number of [ec1c3b]overpower[-] in the final results, gain the following:

choices.outcome: You don’t catch anything other than a brief glimpse of fish. Gain 1 boost.
Catch a small but savory fish; gain 1 health.
Catch a medium-sized fish; gain 1 health and 1 boost.
Catch a large fish; gain item [b]Item 1226[/b].
Catch a monstrosity; gain item [b]Item 1232[/b].

415.text: The flowers on the nearby floating boulder catch your eye. You unearth the vegetation, shaking dirt from its roots. Gain flora [b]Item 1050[/b] and continue.

416.text: After great resistance, the lock snaps open. Roll 5 skill dice; you may reroll each die once. If you have at least 3 of a kind, gain item [b]Item 1193[/b]. Instead, if you have at least one pair, gain item [b]Item 1162[/b]. Otherwise, gain $4.

417.action: [ec1c3b]FORCE[-] the sentient to lead you to their destination
417.text: The brightly lit sentient is easy to track as they guide you through an increasingly dark staircase. They bid you farewell, leaving you alone underground at the end of the secret passage. Go to cave [b]Location 094[/b].

418.text: Several meters underground, you feel a sense of satisfaction and strength. Gain 1 health and 1 boost. You may either stop or continue with an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆2-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result only):

choices.outcome: A solid material halts your progress, and you excavate a large crystal. Gain item [b]Item 1162[/b]. You may either stop or continue with an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆3-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result only):

A rich, dark layer of sediment is prime for deep fungus. Gain item [b]Item 1136[/b]. You may either stop or continue with an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆4-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result):
The earth beneath your feet gives way, sending you tumbling into the depths. Go to cave [b]Location 094[/b].

419.action: [ec1c3b]STUN[-] a sentient as it emerges from the tower
419.text: A translucent pink sentient blinks into existence from one of the lights on the tower. You catch it off guard, slamming it to the ground. They beg for you to let them live, offering a lesson in magic in exchange. Choose one:

choices.outcome: Gain lesson [b]Item 1424[/b].
The allure of the sentient’s life force is too much for you to resist. They blink out of existence, and you lose 1 morale and gain 2 health.

042.action: [ec1c3b]FORCE[-] a returning sentient to explain the contents of the monitors
042.text: Upon returning to the room and finding you inside, the sentient reaches for a hidden alarm. You spring into action, pinning their metallic hand to the desk and daring them to try again. They explain that they’re hired by wealthy sentients to monitor their homes, discouraging thieves and bandits. This particular array shows sentients who own the 6 most powerful magical rings in the world… well, technically 5 now, as a sand centipede ate the sixth and disappeared deep into the planet. The watchman begs for you to help them recover the missing ring. Gain sentient [b]Item 1323[/b] and quest [b]Item 1704[/b] (add the quest horizontally to your supply), then exit to city [b]Location 041[/b].

420.text: You’re lucky this feisty animal’s tusks haven’t fully developed, and you overcome it. If you pay 1 boost, gain item [b]Item 1161[/b] from the carcass. If not, gain item [b]Item 1186[/b].

421.text: You can’t help but take pity on the creature, and it sighs in relief when you demonstrate what you will do. Its carcass tumbles to the ground. You may either eat it to gain 1 health or bury it to gain 1 morale. If you pay 1 boost, also gain item [b]Item 1165[/b].

422.text: As you dig, you’re delighted to find a few coins in the mud. Gain $3. You may either proceed west to [b]Location 615[/b] or perform an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆2-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result only):

choices.outcome: A solid material halts your progress, and you excavate a tiny metal box filled with cards. Gain 1 random card from the Skirm spoiler pack (place it in your reserve, where your entire Skirm collection counts as only 1 reserved card. You can use this type of card from your reserve during [ec1c3b]overpower-[b]DUEL[/b][-] actions, which have further instructions). You may either stop or continue with an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆3-[b]DIG[/b][-] action to go deeper (then read the next result):
The earth beneath your feet gives way, sending you tumbling into the depths. Go to city [b]Location 041[/b].

423.action: [ec1c3b]RAID[-] the structural remains for anything useful
423.text: You find an old pair of pliers. Gain item [b]Item 881[/b] and continue with any action other than [fff349]take-[b]SCAVENGE[/b][-].

424.text: The device is durable… but not that durable. Bits and pieces of circuitry scatter across the ice. Gain item [b]Item 1190[/b].

425.text: You begin to raze the device, and soon you find yourself in a scrap heap of metal and circuitry. The runecaster is now useless, but you’re impressed with how its creators took a naturally occurring phenomenon— runes—and produced a superior synthetic version. Gain 1 boost and your choice of a metal scrap (item [b]Item 1184[/b]) or a circuit board (item [b]Item 1190[/b]).

426.text: The beast is swift and fierce; it is clearly more often the predator, not the prey. You finally defeat it. If you pay 1 boost, gain item [b]Item 1160[/b] from the carcass. If not, gain item [b]Item 1181[/b].

427.text: As soon as you attack, the sentient is upon you in a swirl of well-practiced maneuvers. You gain the edge by working your way out of the water, slicing through the bulbous flowers to create an awkward battleground. Finally, you prevail. Gain either a pair of binoculars (item [b]Item 972[/b]) or one of the sea moon flowers (flora [b]Item 1066[/b]).

428.text: You unearth the vegetation, shaking water and dirt from the roots. A nearby sentient sees what you’ve done and smites you. Gain flora [b]Item 1066[/b] and curse [b]Item 1385[/b].

429.text: Roll 3 skill dice up to 3 times (always rolling all dice); roll 1 extra die if you pay a maximum of 1 boost from a food or flora. Based on number of [ec1c3b]overpower[-] in the final results, gain the following:

choices.outcome: You don’t catch anything other than a brief glimpse of fish. Gain 1 boost.
Catch a small but savory fish; gain 1 health.
Catch a medium-sized fish; gain 1 health and 1 boost.
Catch a large fish; gain item [b]Item 1226[/b].
Catch a monstrosity; gain item [b]Item 1232[/b].

043.text: The worms are too young to fight back, so instead they scatter in all directions. You happen to notice a few of them slip into a small hole to the right. If you want to follow them, go deeper into the planet to cave [b]Location 040[/b]. Otherwise, gain 1 boost.

430.action: [ec1c3b]FORCE[-] your way through the sea spouts
430.text: Your arms and legs churning against the powerful water, you manage to go north to [b]Location 431[/b]. Gain 1 morale.

431.text: The leaf elemental paces before you with mild curiosity. First, gain curse [b]Item 1388[/b]. Then find elemental combat cards [b]Item 1533[/b]–[b]Item 1536[/b] and compile them into a cohesive image. Choose one of those cards and continue to perform an action on it.

432.text: After great resistance, the lock snaps open. Roll 5 skill dice; you may reroll each die once. If you have at least 3 of a kind, gain item [b]Item 1043[/b]. Instead, if you have at least one pair, gain item [b]Item 1162[/b]. Otherwise, gain $4.

433.text: Roll 3 skill dice up to 3 times (always rolling all dice); roll 1 extra die if you pay a maximum of 1 boost from a food or flora. Based on number of [ec1c3b]overpower[-] in the final results, gain the following:

choices.outcome: You don’t catch anything other than a brief glimpse of fish. Gain 1 boost.
Catch a small but savory fish; gain 1 health.
Catch a medium-sized fish; gain 1 health and 1 boost.
Catch a large fish; gain item [b]Item 1226[/b].
Catch a monstrosity; gain item [b]Item 1232[/b].

434.text: You notice movement in the brush, so you set a basic trap and wait for it to spring. Set 1 challenge die to time, morale, or health to represent the trap (read options below). Then roll 3 other challenge dice; if any of them match the set die, gain the resulting benefit once (if none match, gain [ec1c3b]1 overpower[-] instead). These dice can come from the dice pool or the center; return them afterward.

choices.option: TIME (try to study an animal’s behavior)
choices.outcome: You observe the critter for a little while before setting it free. Gain 1 boost on your character and 1 boost on each leaf card.

choices.option: MORALE (try to gain an animal companion)
choices.outcome: The animal is scared but complacent. You will have to work to gain its trust. Gain animal [b]Item 851[/b].

choices.option: HEALTH (try to kill and eat an animal)
choices.outcome: The trap instantly kills the animal. Gain item [b]Item 1217[/b].

435.action: [ec1c3b]CLEAVE[-] the tall stone so it bridges the divide
435.text: The stone crashes across the gap, startling nearby sentients but providing a better way to cross the gap. Gain 1 morale; you may go east to [b]Location 734[/b] or continue.

436.text: You begin to raze the device, and soon you find yourself in a scrap heap of metal and circuitry. The runecaster is now useless, but you’re impressed with how its creators took a naturally occurring phenomenon— runes—and produced a superior synthetic version. Gain 1 boost and your choice of a metal scrap (item [b]Item 1184[/b]) or a circuit board (item [b]Item 1190[/b]).

437.text: In case the symbol has hypnotized the animals, you smash the rune, leaving it unrecognizable. Whether the spell is broken or you simply surprised them, the animals skitter away. Gain 1 morale.

438.text: While it takes some effort to get the boat to shore, the sailor doesn’t put up a fight. They mumble something about another boat to the east while you sail away. Gain vehicle [b]Item 1014[/b] and $4.

439.action: [ec1c3b]FORCE[-] the sentient to give you the book
439.text: The sentient doesn’t resist, but as you walk away, you find yourself right back where you started. Gain 1 boost.

044.text: You are at a huge disadvantage against such a heavily armored leviathan, but you somehow manage to kill it. Gain item [b]Item 1186[/b] from the carcass. If you pay 2 boost, also gain item [b]Item 991[/b].

440.text: The animals buzz angrily at you, focusing their flames on the pile of metal and circuitry. Miraculously, within a few minutes they have reconstructed the device. If these animals were willing to work for you, they could create wonders. Gain 1 boost and [0b955e]1 help[-]; continue.

441.text: Roll 3 skill dice up to 3 times (always rolling all dice); roll 1 extra die if you pay a maximum of 1 boost from a food or flora. Based on number of [ec1c3b]overpower[-] in the final results, gain the following:

choices.outcome: You don’t catch anything other than a brief glimpse of fish. Gain 1 boost.
Catch a small but savory fish; gain 1 health.
Catch a medium-sized fish; gain 1 health and 1 boost.
Catch a large fish; gain item [b]Item 1226[/b].
Catch a monstrosity; gain item [b]Item 1232[/b].

442.action: [ec1c3b]STUN[-] a sentient as it emerges from the tower
442.text: A translucent pink sentient blinks into existence from one of the lights on the tower. You catch it off guard, slamming it to the ground. They beg for you to let them live, offering a lesson in magic in exchange. Choose one:

choices.outcome: Gain lesson [b]Item 1424[/b].
The allure of the sentient’s life force is too much for you to resist. They blink out of existence, and you lose 1 morale and gain 2 health.

443.text: You fight to deter, not to kill. The beast eventually calms down. You try to better understand why they attacked you (gain 1 boost and [a84fc0]1 look[-]); you may continue with a [a84fc0]look-[b]YIELD[/b][-] action.

444.text: You can’t help but take pity on the mortally wounded creature, and it sighs in relief when you demonstrate what you will do. Its carcass tumbles to the ground. You may either eat it to gain 1 health or bury it to gain 1 morale. If you pay 1 boost, also gain item [b]Item 1162[/b].

445.action: [ec1c3b]WEAPONIZE[-] some rocks from the natural arch
445.text: You feel more confident moving forward with a solid rock in one hand. Gain item [b]Item 1179[/b] and continue.

446.text: The gold elemental paces before you with mild curiosity. First, gain curse [b]Item 1393[/b]. Then find elemental combat cards [b]Item 1545[/b]–[b]Item 1548[/b] and compile them into a cohesive image. Choose one of those cards and continue to perform an action on it.

447.text: You notice movement along the shoreline, so you set a basic trap and wait for it to spring. Set 1 challenge die to time, morale, or health to represent the trap (read options below). Then roll 3 other challenge dice; if any of them match the set die, gain the resulting benefit once (if none match, gain [ec1c3b]1 overpower[-] instead). These dice can come from the dice pool or the center; return them afterward.

choices.option: TIME (try to study an animal’s behavior)
choices.outcome: You observe the critter for a little while before setting it free. Gain 1 boost on your character and 1 boost on each circuit card.

choices.option: MORALE (try to gain an animal companion)
choices.outcome: The animal is scared but complacent. You will have to work to gain its trust. Gain animal [b]Item 820[/b].

choices.option: HEALTH (try to kill and eat an animal)
choices.outcome: The trap instantly kills the animal. Gain item [b]Item 1217[/b].

448.text: After great resistance, the lock snaps open. Roll 5 skill dice; you may reroll each die once. If you have at least 3 of a kind, gain item [b]Item 977[/b]. Instead, if you have at least one pair, gain item [b]Item 908[/b]. Otherwise, gain $4.

449.text: Your aim rings true, and the wood sentient tumbles unconscious into the water. You recover their coin purse from the shallows. Gain $6.

choices.outcome: Anyone riding such a vile creature must be evil themselves. You strike swiftly, before they have time to prepare. The sand centipede slithers away into the tunnel after the sentient slides from its steed. You find a purse on the body and a business card advertising an underground taxi service—the sentient was just trying to give you a ride. Gain $7 and lose 1 morale.

You slice through the beast’s tail, sending the sentient tumbling to the ground. You celebrate and wait for your reward, but the sentient is furious—you just cut off their primary mode of transportation and income! They skulk away, leaving you with the still-writhing tail. Gain item [b]Item 1194[/b] and curse [b]Item 1390[/b].

450.text: Roll 3 skill dice up to 3 times (always rolling all dice); roll 1 extra die if you pay a maximum of 1 boost from a food or flora. Based on number of [ec1c3b]overpower[-] in the final results, gain the following:

choices.outcome: You don’t catch anything other than a brief glimpse of fish. Gain 1 boost.
Catch a small but savory fish; gain 1 health.
Catch a medium-sized fish; gain 1 health and 1 boost.
Catch a large fish; gain item [b]Item 1226[/b].
Catch a monstrosity; gain item [b]Item 1232[/b].

451.text: You stab the closest sentient in the back. As they splash into the water, the other soldier pivots and strikes quickly. They have mastered combat with a trident, and you barely defeat them. Roll 2 skill dice and gain all [2694d0]move[-] results. Lose 1 health unless you pay [2694d0]1 move[-]. Gain item [b]Item 1090[/b].

452.action: [ec1c3b]PLUCK[-] the lotus, careful not to damage the roots or petals
452.text: You pluck one of the enigmatic flowers. Gain flora [b]Item 1159[/b].

453.text: Your forearm snags on a piece of broken glass as you crawl through the window. Lose 1 health and go to interior [b]Location 574[/b].

454.text: You begin to raze the device, and soon you find yourself in a scrap heap of metal and circuitry. The runecaster is now useless, but you’re impressed with how its creators took a naturally occurring phenomenon— runes—and produced a superior synthetic version. Gain 1 boost and your choice of a metal scrap (item [b]Item 1184[/b]) or a circuit board (item [b]Item 1190[/b]).

455.action: [ec1c3b]SUBDUE[-] the gatekeeper and enter the gatehouse
455.text: Before the gatekeeper can respond or sound an alarm, you hogtie them, careful to ensure they don’t see who you are. Go to interior [b]Location 575[/b].

456.text: The occupants, a small group of pirates, are bewildered when you crash into their hideout. Fortunately they’re taking a break, so they’re not wearing armor. Gain ability [b]Item 1467[/b] and go to interior [b]Location 576[/b].

457.text: After great resistance, the lock snaps open. Roll 5 skill dice; you may reroll each die once. If you have at least 3 of a kind, gain item [b]Item 942[/b]. Instead, if you have at least one pair, gain item [b]Item 1165[/b]. Otherwise, gain $4.

458.text: The tower lands with a thud, but it holds firm. It provides a makeshift bridge. Go east to [b]Location 764[/b] or go south to [b]Location 457[/b]; either way, continue.

459.text: Sparks fly as you carve a large gash in the ship, revealing precious cargo—your power armor. You may replace your character with the corresponding card ([2694d0]captain[-]: [b]Item 1447[/b]; [a84fc0]navigator[-]: [b]Item 1448[/b]; [fd8c46]scholar[-]: [b]Item 1449[/b]; [0b955e]medic[-]: [b]Item 1450[/b]; [fff349]engineer[-]: [b]Item 1451[/b]; [ec1c3b]marine[-]: [b]Item 1452[/b]); transfer all boost from your original character onto the new card. If you already have your power armor, instead gain 3 boost.

046.text: You patch together an axe. Gain item [b]Item 872[/b].

460.text: A coordinated cry goes up among the shipwrights as you attack; clearly this isn’t the first time they’ve been the target of pirates. One sentient, not wanting to fight, simply hands you a screwdriver and motions for you to leave. Gain item [b]Item 875[/b].

461.text: You can’t help but take pity on the creature, and it sighs in relief when you demonstrate what you will do. Its carcass tumbles to the ground. You may either eat it to gain 1 health or bury it to gain 1 morale. If you pay 1 boost, also gain item [b]Item 1161[/b].

462.action: [ec1c3b]FLEX[-] for the sentient in the hopes they’ll ask you to fight alongside them
462.text: Your display doesn’t seem to impress the sentient, but they shrug and beckon to you. Their plan is to attack the wind sentient head-on. Gain sentient [b]Item 1311[/b].

463.text: The wind elemental paces before you with mild curiosity. First, gain curse [b]Item 1387[/b]. Then find elemental combat cards [b]Item 1529[/b]–[b]Item 1532[/b] and compile them into a cohesive image. Choose one of those cards and continue to perform an action on it.

464.text: The meek cartographers gathered inside are more protective of their precious maps than their own well-being, and they don’t fight back at all. With the daylight fading, they offer you a pair of night-vision goggles if you’ll leave them to their parchments. Gain item [b]Item 945[/b].

465.text: There’s something toxic about this area. You strategically place rocks around the glow to set up a controlled burn, then you light it on fire. Green sparks dance in the dark, attracting a small group of locals, one of whom thanks you profusely for removing the blight from their land. They shower you with praise and a purse of coins. Gain $15 and 1 morale.

466.action: [ec1c3b]CHARGE[-] through the gap, throwing punches this way and that
466.text: You realize how silly you’ll look if there’s no one on the other side of the gap… but your hunch was right, as you turn around to find two unconscious bandits. You bind them so they can’t bother other travelers. Gain item [b]Item 1209[/b] and go north to [b]Location 467[/b].

467.text: After great resistance, the lock snaps open. Roll 6 skill dice; you may reroll each die once. If you have at least 3 of a kind, gain item [b]Item 1037[/b]. Instead, if you have at least one pair, gain item [b]Item 1164[/b]. Otherwise, gain $4.

468.text: The rocky soil makes digging difficult, but you scrape deep enough to see the underside of the lorestone, which is studded with green crystals. Gain item [b]Item 1163[/b].

469.text: You begin to raze the device, and soon you find yourself in a scrap heap of metal and circuitry. The runecaster is now useless, but you’re impressed with how its creators took a naturally occurring phenomenon—runes—and produced a superior synthetic version. Gain 1 boost and your choice of a metal scrap (item [b]Item 1184[/b]) or a circuit board (item [b]Item 1190[/b]).

047.action: [ec1c3b]HUNT[-] the sand centipede while the hunters hold it at bay
047.text: The hunters commend you on finishing the task, and they invite you to join them on the next hunt. They also show you how to harvest the best bones from the animal, leaving the carcass and its soulstone behind out of respect for nature. Gain item [b]Item 1186[/b] and sentient [b]Item 1333[/b] (the other sentient scouts ahead).

470.text: The beast, gentle as it may seem in the waning sunset, is built for battle. Gain item [b]Item 1186[/b]. If you pay 1 boost, also gain item [b]Item 1165[/b].

471.text: After great resistance, the lock snaps open. Roll 5 skill dice; you may reroll each die once. If you have at least 3 of a kind, gain vehicle [b]Item 957[/b]. Instead, if you have at least one pair, gain item [b]Item 857[/b]. Otherwise, gain $4.

472.text: Roll 3 skill dice up to 3 times (always rolling all dice); roll 1 extra die if you pay a maximum of 1 boost from a food or flora. Based on number of [ec1c3b]overpower[-] in the final results, gain the following:

choices.outcome: You don’t catch anything other than a brief glimpse of fish. Gain 1 boost.
Catch a small but savory fish; gain 1 health.
Catch a medium-sized fish; gain 1 health and 1 boost.
Catch a large fish; gain item [b]Item 1226[/b].
Catch a monstrosity; gain item [b]Item 1232[/b].

473.text: The guards easily dodge your aggression and welcome you into the City of Leaves. Go to city [b]Location 526[/b].

474.text: You begin to raze the device, and soon you find yourself in a scrap heap of metal and circuitry. The runecaster is now useless, but you’re impressed with how its creators took a naturally occurring phenomenon—runes— and produced a superior synthetic version. Gain 1 boost and your choice of a metal scrap (item [b]Item 1184[/b]) or a circuit board (item [b]Item 1190[/b]).

475.text: The creature assumes you’re playing a game until it’s too late. As you prepare to strike the killing blow, a much larger version of the animal bullrushes into you (lose 1 health). Continue immediately with an [ec1c3b]overpower-◆6-[b]DEFEND[/b][-] action, after which you gain item [b]Item 1164[/b].

476.text: As you strike the first blow, a root rises from the island and attempts to strike back at you. A short battle ensues between you and this aggressive tree. Roll 2 skill dice and gain all [ec1c3b]overpower[-] results. Lose 1 health unless you pay [ec1c3b]1 overpower[-]. Gain flora [b]Item 1058[/b] and curse [b]Item 1388[/b].

477.action: [ec1c3b]HUNT[-] the massive beast lurking below the surface
477.text: The water creates the false illusion that the creature is nearby; it’s actually deeper underwater. Either go underwater to [b]Location 642[/b] (where you ignore hypoxia damage on arrival) or gain 1 boost and continue.

478.text: You begin to raze the device, and soon you find yourself in a scrap heap of metal and circuitry. The runecaster is now useless, but you’re impressed with how its creators took a naturally occurring phenomenon—runes— and produced a superior synthetic version. Gain 1 boost and your choice of a metal scrap (item [b]Item 1184[/b]) or a circuit board (item [b]Item 1190[/b]).

479.text: It’s difficult for you to fight while treading water, particularly against a sentient who was born in the ocean. Somehow you prevail. Gain item [b]Item 940[/b] and $5.

048.text: You dash down the passage and just barely manage to grab a spike and jam it into a crack in the rocks. The animal jerks to a halt. It can’t turn around in such a tight space, so you act quickly, opening a long gash along its belly. If you pay 2 boost, gain item [b]Item 991[/b] from inside the carcass. If not, gain item [b]Item 1160[/b].

480.text: You can’t help but take pity on the creature, and it sighs in relief when you demonstrate what you will do. Its carcass slumps to the waves. You may either eat it to gain 1 health or help it sink to a watery grave to gain 1 morale. If you pay 1 boost, also gain item [b]Item 1162[/b].

481.text: Roll 3 skill dice up to 3 times (always rolling all dice); roll 1 extra die if you pay a maximum of 1 boost from a food or flora. Based on number of [ec1c3b]overpower[-] in the final results, gain the following:

choices.outcome: You don’t catch anything other than a brief glimpse of fish. Gain 1 boost.
Catch a small but savory fish; gain 1 health.
Catch a medium-sized fish; gain 1 health and 1 boost.
Catch a large fish; gain item [b]Item 1226[/b].
Catch a monstrosity; gain item [b]Item 1232[/b].

482.text: As helpless as the sentients are, they far outnumber you, and you almost fall off the rock several times before you manage to become king of the hill. As the sentients flounder in the water, you notice a map wedged in a crag. Gain item [b]Item 1252[/b] and 1 random skill.

483.text: You take advantage of the animal’s vulnerable position and quickly dispatch it. If you pay 1 boost, gain item [b]Item 1162[/b] from the carcass. If not, gain item [b]Item 1184[/b].

484.action: [ec1c3b]STUN[-] a sentient as it emerges from the tower
484.text: A translucent pink sentient blinks into existence from one of the lights on the tower. You catch it off guard, slamming it to the ground. They beg for you to let them live, offering a lesson in magic in exchange. Choose one:

choices.outcome: Gain lesson [b]Item 1424[/b].
The allure of the sentient’s life force is too much for you to resist. They blink out of existence, and you lose 1 morale and gain 2 health.

485.text: After great resistance, the lock snaps open. Roll 5 skill dice; you may reroll each die once. If you have at least 3 of a kind, gain item [b]Item 1089[/b]. Instead, if you have at least one pair, gain item [b]Item 1162[/b]. Otherwise, gain $4.

486.text: You notice movement in the sand, so you set a basic trap and wait for it to spring. Set 1 challenge die to time, morale, or health to represent the trap (read options below). Then roll 3 other challenge dice; if any of them match the set die, gain the resulting benefit once (if none match, gain [ec1c3b]1 overpower[-] instead). These dice can come from the dice pool or the center; return them afterward.

choices.option: TIME (try to study an animal’s behavior)
choices.outcome: You observe the critter for a little while before setting it free. Gain 1 boost on your character and 1 boost on each sinew card.

choices.option: MORALE (try to gain an animal companion)
choices.outcome: The animal is scared but complacent. You will have to work to gain its trust. Gain animal [b]Item 826[/b].

choices.option: HEALTH (try to kill and eat an animal)
choices.outcome: The trap instantly kills the animal. Gain item [b]Item 1217[/b].

487.action: [ec1c3b]CUT[-] through a rope and swing across the gap
487.text: You feel like an adventurer of old, swinging from tree to tree. Except in this case the tree is a solid stone wall, and you feel something crack in your shoulder. Lose 1 health and go to [b]Location 276[/b].

488.text: After great resistance, the lock snaps open. Roll 5 skill dice; you may reroll each die once. If you have at least 3 of a kind, gain item [b]Item 983[/b]. Instead, if you have at least one pair, gain item [b]Item 1162[/b]. Otherwise, gain $4.

489.text: With the number of animals increasing every minute, the odds are on your side. Plus, the animals don’t seem all that interested in helping each other. Gain item [b]Item 1186[/b]. If you pay 1 boost, also gain item [b]Item 1161[/b].

049.text: As you do, you hear a great cry of agony, and the entire cave starts to move. This is no cave—you’re inside an animal! You hack desperately at any vital organs you can find while avoiding the dangerous spikes, and finally the beast goes limp. Roll 3 skill dice and gain all [2694d0]move[-] results. Lose 1 health unless you pay [2694d0]1 move[-]. Gain item [b]Item 1160[/b] and go to cave [b]Location 043[/b].

490.action: [ec1c3b]PLUCK[-] the lotus, careful not to damage the roots or petals
490.text: You pluck the largest of the resilient flowers. Gain flora [b]Item 1159[/b].

491.text: Sparks fly as you carve a large gash in the ship, revealing precious cargo—your power armor. You may replace your character with the corresponding card ([2694d0]captain[-]: [b]Item 1447[/b]; [a84fc0]navigator[-]: [b]Item 1448[/b]; [fd8c46]scholar[-]: [b]Item 1449[/b]; [0b955e]medic[-]: [b]Item 1450[/b]; [fff349]engineer[-]: [b]Item 1451[/b]; [ec1c3b]marine[-]: [b]Item 1452[/b]); transfer all boost from your original character onto the new card. If you already have your power armor, instead gain 3 boost.

492.action: [ec1c3b]CUT[-] into the snow one step at a time, providing purchase for the long journey downhill
492.text: This method is exhausting, though entirely stable—you’re never in danger of slipping or sliding. Go north to [b]Location 351[/b].

493.text: It’s a pity to kill such a beautiful creature. If you pay 1 boost, gain item [b]Item 1161[/b] from the carcass. If not, gain item [b]Item 1211[/b].

494.text: After great resistance, the lock snaps open. Roll 5 skill dice; you may reroll each die once. If you have at least 3 of a kind, gain item [b]Item 1083[/b]. Instead, if you have at least one pair, gain item [b]Item 1165[/b]. Otherwise, gain $4.

495.text: Its days as a bridge are long gone, but the rope provides a much easier way to descend to the valley below. Gain 1 boost. You may go north down the cliff to [b]Location 354[/b].

496.action: [ec1c3b]PLUCK[-] the lotus from the snow, careful not to damage the roots or petals
496.text: You pluck the striking flower. Gain flora [b]Item 1159[/b].

497.action: [ec1c3b]CLEAVE[-] a standing stone and watch it crash toward the warehouse
497.text: The boulder makes the biggest splash you’ve ever seen, catching the attention of dockside workers. You use the opportunity to slip into the building. Go to interior [b]Location 578[/b].

498.text: The sinew elemental paces before you with mild curiosity. First, gain curse [b]Item 1392[/b]. Then find elemental combat cards [b]Item 1553[/b]–[b]Item 1556[/b] and compile them into a cohesive image. Choose one of those cards and continue to perform an action on it.

499.text: The result of you entering the building is based on the current occupancy status. Proceed if you already know this status; if not, roll a challenge die to randomly determine the status:

choices.option: No one is home (blank challenge die)
choices.outcome: Go to interior [b]Location 579[/b]; on your next turn, you may not choose the [fd8c46]engage-[b]DEFUSE[/b][-] action there.

The house is occupied. Your aggressive entrance sends the family running (gain curse [b]Item 1394[/b] and go to interior [b]Location 579[/b]; on your next turn, you may not choose the [fd8c46]engage-[b]DEFUSE[/b][-] action there.).

050.action: [ec1c3b]SMASH[-] boulders to try to form a crude stairway toward the barrier
050.text: Your attempts at brute force are fruitless. Gain 1 boost and continue.

500.action: [ec1c3b]CLEAVE[-] the tower so it topples over the lava field
500.text: Your feet burn as you cross the lava to reach the tower; suffer 1 heat damage. The tower lands with a thud, providing a cushion against the scalding lava. You may go north to [b]Location 359[/b].

501.text: Reaching the device requires you to move across a lava field; suffer 1 heat damage. You begin to raze the device, and soon you find yourself in a scrap heap of metal and circuitry. The runecaster is now useless, but you’re impressed with how its creators took a naturally occurring phenomenon— runes—and produced a superior synthetic version. Gain 1 boost and your choice of a metal scrap (item [b]Item 1184[/b]) or a circuit board (item [b]Item 1190[/b]).

502.text: After great resistance, the lock snaps open. Roll 5 skill dice; you may reroll each die once. If you have at least 3 of a kind, gain item [b]Item 1036[/b]. Instead, if you have at least one pair, gain item [b]Item 1164[/b]. Otherwise, gain $4.

503.text: The orb is surprisingly durable, but you manage to crack and break it. Gain item [b]Item 1194[/b].

504.action: [ec1c3b]FIGHT[-] one of the parties involved in the altercation

choices.option: Fight alongside the pirates as they attack the sailboat
choices.outcome: In the heat of battle, the pirates barely notice that you’re not one of them. When the smoke clears, they’re delighted by your gumption. You may sail west with them to a treasure chest (go to [b]Location 502[/b] or [b]Location 503[/b]).

choices.option: Attack the pirate ship while its crew is focused on the sailboat
choices.outcome: You carve through the pirates with relative ease until they’re forced to surrender. They offer you a treasure map if you leave them be. Gain item [b]Item 1260[/b]; they highlight your current location (view page 17 in the Book of Vantages; you are in region D).

choices.option: Pay [ec1c3b]1 overpower[-] to defend the sailboat from the pirates
choices.outcome: The pirates seem delighted that someone is putting up a good fight for a change, and they eventually leave you be and fly away to the east. You are a hero among the sailors, and one of them is quite taken with you. Gain vehicle [b]Item 1156[/b] and sentient [b]Item 1360[/b].

505.action: [ec1c3b]DUEL[-] the sentient in a card and dice game
505.text: The gold sentient eagerly reveals a vast collection of cards. Place card [b]Location 798[/b] on the table (it’s a large card) and follow its instructions, using card [b]Item 1521[/b] as the opponent’s card.Variant: Quick Skirm (ignore card 798).

506.text: The tower lands with a splash, floating on the surf. It provides a makeshift path to move further out into the sea. You may go north to [b]Location 365[/b].

507.action: [ec1c3b]TRAP[-] some fish while the tide is low
507.text: You notice movement in the seaweed, so you set a basic trap and wait for it to spring. Set 1 challenge die to time, morale, or health to represent the trap (read options below). Then roll 3 other challenge dice; if any of them match the set die, gain the resulting benefit once (if none match, gain [ec1c3b]1 overpower[-] instead). These dice can come from the dice pool or the center; return them afterward.

choices.option: TIME (try to study an animal’s behavior)
choices.outcome: You observe the critter for a little while before setting it free. Gain 1 boost on your character and 1 boost on each wind card.

choices.option: MORALE (try to gain an animal companion)
choices.outcome: The animal is scared but complacent. You will have to work to gain its trust. Gain animal [b]Item 806[/b].

choices.option: HEALTH (try to kill and eat an animal)
choices.outcome: The trap instantly kills the animal. Gain item [b]Item 1226[/b].

508.action: [ec1c3b]FIGHT[-] one of the parties involved in the altercation

choices.option: Defend the animal from the poachers
choices.outcome: The poachers are wealthy tourists hunting for sport, and they weren’t expecting their prey to fight back. They flee at the first sign of adversity. The animal sidles up to you in gratitude. Gain animal [b]Item 847[/b].

choices.option: Hunt the animal with the poachers
choices.outcome: The massive beast struggles against the onslaught, lashing out with sharp tendrils. Roll 3 skill dice and gain all [2694d0]move[-] results. Lose 1 health unless you pay [2694d0]1 move[-]. The hunters then sail away— they were hunting for sport, not for necessity. There’s nothing you can do as the animal slowly sinks under the waves. You salvage some viscous fluid from its tendrils; lose 1 morale and gain item [b]Item 861[/b].

509.text: Sparks fly as you carve a large gash in the ship, revealing precious cargo—your power armor. You may replace your character with the corresponding card ([2694d0]captain[-]: [b]Item 1447[/b]; [a84fc0]navigator[-]: [b]Item 1448[/b]; [fd8c46]scholar[-]: [b]Item 1449[/b]; [0b955e]medic[-]: [b]Item 1450[/b]; [fff349]engineer[-]: [b]Item 1451[/b]; [ec1c3b]marine[-]: [b]Item 1452[/b]); transfer all boost from your original character onto the new card. If you already have your power armor, instead gain 3 boost.

510.action: [ec1c3b]RAID[-] the cottage of its largest item
510.text: You muster the strength to carry the jet pack. Gain vehicle [b]Item 1253[/b] and quietly lurch outside (exit to [b]Location 476[/b]).

511.text: As you reach for the flora, you realize that there is one fewer flora than a few seconds ago. Gain flora [b]Item 1073[/b] and continue with the [a84fc0]look-[b]SOLVE[/b][-] action with a cost reduced by 1.

512.text: Roll 3 skill dice up to 3 times (always rolling all dice); roll 1 extra die if you pay a maximum of 1 boost from a food or flora. Based on number of [ec1c3b]overpower[-] in the final results, gain the following:

choices.outcome: You don’t catch anything other than a brief glimpse of fish. Gain 1 boost.
Catch a small but savory fish; gain 1 health.
Catch a medium-sized fish; gain 1 health and 1 boost.
Catch a large fish; gain item [b]Item 1226[/b].
Catch a monstrosity; gain item [b]Item 1232[/b].

513.action: [ec1c3b]CUT[-] the sentient loose of their tethers
513.text: The sentient’s indifference turns to fear, and they beg you to stop. “This is my job, my responsibility!” they scream. They curse you for your ignorance. Gain curse [b]Item 1395[/b] and 1 boost.

514.text: With a mighty heave, you tear the amulet from its axis. When you do, the light fades from the lorestones. Gain item [b]Item 1035[/b] and curse [b]Item 1393[/b].

515.action: [ec1c3b]DIG[-] up the area around the gateway
515.text: Choose one based on your circumstances:

choices.option: If you hid an item near the gateway before entering
choices.outcome: Transfer that item to your grid, fill it with boost, and continue.

choices.option: If you did not hide an item near the gateway before entering
choices.outcome: Gain 1 boost and continue.

516.text: One hundred years of growth resulted in a thick trunk, but eventually it comes crashing down. Gain item [b]Item 1150[/b].

517.action: [ec1c3b]RELIEVE[-] the fire sentient of their duty
517.text: The smelter looks up from their work with both literal and metaphorical fire in their eyes. “One hundred years,” they say, “and this is the first time you checked in? I spent my life building this ship for you. One hundred years! The way I see it, this ship is mine—if you want it, you’ll have to take it from me.” You may either leave the ship in the possession of the fire sentient (gain 1 morale and 1 boost) or continue with a [fff349]take-◆4-[b]HIJACK[/b][-] action to take it with force (gain vehicle [b]Item 1033[/b]).

518.text: A towering windmill seems designed to generate wind, not move as the result of it. The sentients explain that they are responsible for planting seeds across the world. Choose one:

choices.option: Use your strength to assist the windmill employees
choices.outcome: Gain 2 morale.

choices.option: Duel an off-duty sentient in a card and dice game
choices.outcome: The wind sentient’s eyes light up at the prospect of playing their favorite game. Place card [b]Location 798[/b] on the table (it’s a large card) and follow its instructions, using card [b]Item 1518[/b] as the opponent’s card.Variant: Quick Skirm (ignore card 798).

519.text: The shoemaker grumbles about their competition to the southeast. Choose one:

choices.outcome: Gain quest [b]Item 1685[/b] (add it horizontally to your supply).

choices.option: Pay $3 to gain a snow-friendly shoe
choices.outcome: Gain item [b]Item 933[/b].

choices.option: Duel the shoemaker in a card and dice game
choices.outcome: The wood sentient’s eyes light up at the prospect of playing their favorite game. Place card [b]Location 798[/b] on the table (it’s a large card) and follow its instructions, using card [b]Item 1528[/b] as the opponent’s card.Variant: Quick Skirm (ignore card 798).

520.action: [ec1c3b]DUEL[-] the nearby sentient in a card and dice game
520.text: The energy sentient’s eyes light up at the prospect of playing their favorite game. Place card [b]Location 798[/b] on the table (it’s a large card) and follow its instructions, using card [b]Item 1519[/b] as the opponent’s card.Variant: Quick Skirm (ignore card 798).

521.action: [ec1c3b]DUEL[-] the nearby sentient in a card and dice game
521.text: The circuit sentient’s eyes light up at the prospect of playing their favorite game. Place card [b]Location 798[/b] on the table (it’s a large card) and follow its instructions, using card [b]Item 1517[/b] as the opponent’s card.Variant: Quick Skirm (ignore card 798).

522.action: [ec1c3b]DUEL[-] the nearby sentient in a card and dice game
522.text: The metal sentient’s eyes light up at the prospect of playing their favorite game. Place card [b]Location 798[/b] on the table (it’s a large card) and follow its instructions, using card [b]Item 1523[/b] as the opponent’s card.Variant: Quick Skirm (ignore card 798).

523.text: Stone sentients relax in the rock-tumbling spa. Choose one:

choices.option: Relax in the spa as smooth, warm rocks toss against your exposed skin
choices.outcome: Roll 5 skill dice. Gain 1 morale for each [0b955e]help[-] result, then gain 1 health.

choices.option: Smash rocks over a sentient’s back, which is a relaxing massage for them
choices.outcome: The sentient is so content afterward that they offer to show you around. Gain sentient [b]Item 1349[/b].

choices.option: Duel a vacationing sentient in a card and dice game
choices.outcome: The stone sentient’s eyes light up at the prospect of playing their favorite game. Place card [b]Location 798[/b] on the table (it’s a large card) and follow its instructions, using card [b]Item 1525[/b] as the opponent’s card.Variant: Quick Skirm (ignore card 798).

524.text: A group of sentients lounges in the hunting lodge, sharing stories of the biggest animal they ever bagged. Choose one:

choices.option: Hunt an animal with a pair of sinew sentients, one of whom takes quickly to you
choices.outcome: Gain sentient [b]Item 1333[/b] and quest [b]Item 1649[/b] (add the quest horizontally to your supply).

choices.option: Flex to impress a new sinew sentient friend
choices.outcome: Gain sentient [b]Item 1330[/b].

choices.option: Duel one of the sentients in a card and dice game
choices.outcome: The sinew sentient’s eye lights up at the prospect of playing their favorite game. Place card [b]Location 798[/b] on the table (it’s a large card) and follow its instructions, using card [b]Item 1522[/b] as the opponent’s card.Variant: Quick Skirm (ignore card 798).

525.text: A fire sentient grins as a tattoo artist etches an intricate design into their metal frame. Choose one:

choices.option: Burn your initials onto the sentient’s arm
choices.outcome: The sentient explains that they earned this tattoo by completing a test of endurance. Gain 1 morale and 1 boost.

choices.option: Grit your teeth as the tattoo artist etches a small symbol into your shoulder
choices.outcome: The fire sentients are delighted that someone with flesh as soft as yours would accept an honorary tattoo. Gain item [b]Item 1241[/b] (also, if you have it, lose the fire curse) and lose 1 health.

choices.option: Duel a nearby sentient in a card and dice game
choices.outcome: The fire sentient’s eyes light up at the prospect of playing their favorite game. Place card [b]Location 798[/b] on the table (it’s a large card) and follow its instructions, using card [b]Item 1520[/b] as the opponent’s card.Variant: Quick Skirm (ignore card 798).

526.action: [ec1c3b]DUEL[-] the nearby sentient in a card and dice game
526.text: The leaf sentient’s eyes light up at the prospect of playing their favorite game. Place card [b]Location 798[/b] on the table (it’s a large card) and follow its instructions, using card [b]Item 1526[/b] as the opponent’s card.Variant: Quick Skirm (ignore card 798).

527.text: A dock worker invites you to step into the boat as the waves gently lap against it. Choose one:

choices.option: Force the sentient to guide you to the Pearl of the Island
choices.outcome: Go northwest to [b]Location 733[/b].

choices.option: Coerce the sentient to teach you to fish
choices.outcome: Gain ability [b]Item 1461[/b] and 1 morale.
Roll 3 skill dice up to 3 times (always rolling all dice). Gain 1 health per [ec1c3b]overpower[-] in the final result.

choices.option: Duel a nearby sentient in a card and dice game
choices.outcome: The sentient’s eyes light up at the prospect of playing their favorite game. Place card [b]Location 798[/b] on the table (it’s a large card) and follow its instructions.Variant: Quick Skirm (ignore card 798).
